text: CubETH is a Swiss satellite project. As of 2015, it is being developed by several Swiss academic and industrial partners. The spacecraft is a single unit CubeSat and therefore 10 x 10 x 10 cm (3.9 x 3.9 x 3.9 in) in size. Its main goal is to prove the feasibility of attitude and orbit determination with low cost commercial off-the-shelf GNSS-receivers.

Jacob Blankenship (born in Blacklick, Ohio) is an American pole vaulter. A standout at Gahanna Lincoln High School, he set Ohio state records and won bronze at the 2011 World Youth Championships. Competing for the University of Tennessee, he became a top collegiate vaulter, earning multiple SEC and NCAA podium finishes. He won the Penn Relays twice (2013, 2014) and claimed gold at the 2014 NACAC U23 Championships. His personal best of 5.80 m (19 ft ¼ in) ranks among the top collegiate performances. He also placed third at the 2015 USA Outdoor Championships.

The king of Rome (Lang-la) was the chief magistrate of the Roman Kingdom. According to legend, the first king of Rome was Romulus, who founded the city in 753 BC upon the Palatine Hill. Seven legendary kings are said to have ruled Rome until 509 BC, when the last king was overthrown. These kings ruled for an average of 35 years.

The kings after Romulus were not known to be dynasts and no reference is made to the hereditary principle until after the fifth king Tarquinius Priscus. Consequently, some have assumed that the Tarquins' attempt to institute a hereditary monarchy over this conjectured earlier elective monarchy resulted in the formation of the Republic.

Dardanella was a prominent toneel (theatre) troupe in the Dutch East Indies (now Indonesia), active from 1926 to 1936. Founded by Willy A. Piedro, it adapted Hollywood films and original stories into Malay-language productions, blending European theatrical styles with local themes. The troupe starred actors like Devi Dja and Tan Tjeng Bok, earning acclaim for its performances. Later joined by playwright Andjar Asmara, Dardanella shifted toward more serious dramas. Though it disbanded after a failed film project, its members influenced early Indonesian cinema. The troupe was praised for promoting national unity through Malay and diverse cultural representation.
River City Labs is an Australian coworking space and accelerator founded in 2012.

Martina Carraro (born 21 June 1993) is an Italian competitive swimmer specializing in breaststroke. She has won medals at the World and European Championships, including bronze in the 100 m breaststroke at the 2019 World Aquatics Championships. Carraro has also competed in the Olympics and holds multiple Italian records.
Malcolm Lockyer (1923–1976) was a British composer and conductor known for his film scores and work with the BBC orchestras.

Nicole Bilderback (born June 10, 1975) is a Korean-born American actress, known for her recurring guest roles on the television programs Dark Angel and Dawson's Creek, and the films Bring It On and Bad Girls From Valley High (aka A Fate Totally Worse than Death). She also was one of the Cordettes, Cordelia Chase's friends, on the Buffy the Vampire Slayer episode "The Wish" and the Unaired Buffy pilot. She also starred in a few episodes in season 6 of The Fresh Prince of Bel Air as Ashley's friend.
HD 37974 (or R 126) a variable B[e] hypergiant in the Large Magellanic Cloud. It is surrounded by an unexpected dust disk.

Millstatt am See is a town in Carinthia, Austria, located on the northern shore of Lake Millstatt. Known for its historic Benedictine abbey and scenic alpine setting, it has evolved from a medieval monastic center into a popular tourist destination. The municipality includes the villages of Obermillstatt, Matzelsdorf, and Laubendorf.

Matt Seers (born 28 June 1974) is an Australian former professional rugby league footballer who played as a fullback, centre, or five-eighth. He spent most of his career with the North Sydney Bears (1993–99) and later played for the Wests Tigers, Wakefield Trinity, and Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks. Seers also represented New South Wales in State of Origin.

Oak Grove is a city in Christian County, Kentucky, United States. Located along the Tennessee border near Fort Campbell, it was incorporated in 1974. As of the 2020 census, its population was 7,931. The city is part of the Clarksville, Tennessee metropolitan area.

Kamaha Castle (鎌刃城) was a 15th-century *yamashiro*-style Japanese castle in Maibara, Shiga Prefecture. Built by the Hori clan, it guarded the Nakasendō highway and played a key role in conflicts between the Rokkaku, Asai, and Oda clans. Abandoned in 1574, its ruins are now a National Historic Site.

Steve Kmetko (born February 16, 1953) is an American broadcast journalist and entertainment reporter. He worked as a news anchor in Louisville and Grand Rapids before transitioning to entertainment journalism at KCBS in Los Angeles. He later became a prominent co-anchor for *E! News Daily* and *E! News Weekend*, covering major Hollywood events. Kmetko has also contributed to *The Advocate* and appeared in films like *Zoolander* (2001). He publicly came out as gay in 1999.

The karbovanets or karbovanet (Lang-uk, plural: карбованці, karbovantsi for 2–4, or карбованців, karbovantsiv for 5 or more), also known as kupon (Lang-uk, plural: купони, kupony) or coupon, has been a distinct unit of currency in Ukraine during three separate periods of the 20th century. It is also a predecessor currency of today's Ukrainian hryvnia.

The karbovanets was subdivided into one hundred kopiykas, but no denominations in kopiykas have ever been issued.

In the ISO 4217 standard, the official name is spelled as karbovanet, while English version of the National Bank of Ukraine's website refers to it as karbovanets.

*The Meaning of Life* (also known as *The Meaning of Life with Gay Byrne*) was an Irish interview television series hosted by Gay Byrne. Airing on RTÉ One from 2009 to 2016, the show explored philosophical and religious themes through conversations with notable guests. It ran for 12 series and 50 episodes, drawing an average of 247,000 viewers. Critics noted its increasingly religious tone, with some interviews sparking debate over faith and existential questions.

Patricia Derian (August 12, 1929 – May 20, 2016) was an American civil rights activist and diplomat who served as the first Assistant Secretary of State for Human Rights and Humanitarian Affairs under President Jimmy Carter (1977–1981). A former nurse and civil rights organizer in Mississippi, she became a vocal advocate for human rights, opposing authoritarian regimes in Argentina, South Korea, and Iran. Her efforts included testifying against Argentina's military junta and supporting dissidents like Kim Dae-jung. She died in Chapel Hill, North Carolina, at age 86.

Opendatasoft is a French company that offers data sharing software. Based in Paris, it also has a subsidiary in Boston (United States) and an office in Nantes (France).

Opendatasoft has developed a tool for sharing and reusing the data of companies and public administrations. Its software lets you organize, share, and visualize any type of data. The software can be used by both private companies and public entities

Climax is a village in Kalamazoo County in the U.S. state of Michigan. The population was 767 at the 2010 census. The village is located within Climax Township on the northern boundary with Charleston Township, and is roughly 12 miles (19 km) west of Battle Creek and 16 miles (26 km) east of Kalamazoo. Climax shares a school district with the neighboring town of Scotts.

Sir Robin Buchanan Nicholson, FRS FREng (born 12 August 1934) is a British industrial metallurgist and academic, who served as Chief Scientific Adviser, Cabinet Office, from 1983 to 1985. He then joined the board of Rolls-Royce plc, where he served until 2005. He was also a non-executive board member of BP plc and Pilkington plc.
HD 37974 (RMC 126) is a massive, luminous B[e] supergiant star in the Large Magellanic Cloud, known for its strong stellar winds and a surrounding dusty disc.

Dan Beach Bradley (18 July 1804 – 23 June 1873) was an American Protestant missionary to Siam from 1835 until his death. He is credited with numerous firsts, including, bringing the first Thai-script printing press to Siam, publishing the first Thai newspaper and monolingual Thai dictionary, performing the first surgery in Siam, and introducing Western medicine and technology.

Madden NFL 25 is an American football sports video game based on the National Football League and published by EA Sports. It was released for PlayStation 3 and Xbox 360 in August 2013. Instead of numbering it Madden NFL 14 with the year like in previous versions, the "25" in the title refers to the 25th anniversary of the Madden NFL series. The eighth-generation console versions of Madden NFL 25 are the very first games to run on EA Sports's Ignite game engine. However, the seventh generation versions still run on EA's previous game engine, Impact. The seventh-generation versions featured former Detroit Lions running back Barry Sanders on the cover, while the eighth-generation versions featured Minnesota Vikings running back Adrian Peterson, and were released as launch titles for the PlayStation 4 and Xbox One in November 2013.

Sheikh Ahmad bin Ali bin Abdullah bin Jassim bin Mohammed Al Thani (Lang-ar; 1922 – 25 November 1977) was the Emir of Qatar who ruled from 1960 to 1972. Qatar's financial status witnessed significant improvements during his reign as a result of the enrichment and discovery of several new oil fields. Qatar also gained its independence as a sovereign state in September 1971 under his rule. He was deposed in February 1972 by his cousin, Khalifa bin Hamad Al Thani.

Alimi Ballard (born October 17, 1977) is an American actor known for his roles in *Numb3rs* (2005–2010), *Sabrina, the Teenage Witch* (1997–1999), and *Dark Angel* (2000–2001). He has also appeared in *Loving*, *The City*, and *CSI: Crime Scene Investigation*.
Remnantz is a country house in Marlow in Buckinghamshire. It is listed Grade II* on the National Heritage List for England.

Charles Boarman (December 24, 1795 – September 13, 1879) was a rear admiral in the United States Navy, serving from 1811 to 1876. Born in Bryantown, Maryland, he was educated at Georgetown University before joining the Navy as a midshipman. He saw action in the War of 1812 aboard the brig USS Jefferson on Lake Ontario.  

Boarman later commanded multiple ships, including USS Weazel, USS Java, and USS Brandywine, and served in the Mediterranean, West Indies, and Brazil squadrons, participating in anti-piracy operations. From 1852 to 1855, he was commandant of the Brooklyn Navy Yard, overseeing the outfitting of Commodore Matthew C. Perry's Japan expedition.  

Despite being a slaveholding Southerner, he remained loyal to the Union during the Civil War, freeing his slaves and supporting West Virginia's secession from Virginia. Promoted to rear admiral in 1876, he retired as the longest-serving officer in the Navy at the time. He died in Martinsburg, West Virginia, where his family home, the Boarman House, remains a historic landmark.
Cristián Caro Cordero (born 1942) is a Chilean prelate of the Catholic Church who served as Archbishop of Puerto Montt from 2001 until his resignation in 2018.

What Wild Ecstasy: The Rise and Fall of the Sexual Revolution is a 1997 book about the sexual revolution by John Heidenry. The book received mixed reviews. It was described as interesting and Heidenry was complimented for his discussions of figures such as Bob Guccione, Hugh Hefner, Larry Flynt, and Reuben Sturman. However, he was criticized for his research methods. He was accused of plagiarism because of the use he made of material by other writers, receiving criticism in particular from the journalist Philip Nobile. Critics also wrote that he focused disproportionately on pornography and gave insufficient attention to feminism and women's issues.

Tre Motetter (Three Motets), FS 139, Op. 55, is a composition for unaccompanied choir by Carl Nielsen. It is a setting of three quotations in Latin from different psalms chosen by the composer and his wife Anne Marie Carl-Nielsen. The three motets, Afflictus sum, Dominus regit me and Benedictus Dominus were first performed on 11 April 1930 at the Ny Carlsberg Glyptotek by Mogens Wöldike and the Palestrina choir, to whom they are dedicated. Among the composer's last works, they were published in 1931 by the Skandinavisk Musikforlag in Copenhagen.

Kwai Tsing Theatre (Chinese: 葵青劇院) is a major performance venue in Kwai Chung, New Territories, Hong Kong. It is located in Kwai Fong, near Kwai Fong MTR station. It was built by the Regional Council and opened on 18 November 1999 to provide a medium-size venue for performances. It has an 899-seat auditorium, a black box theatre, and various smaller function rooms.

Alnus glutinosa, commonly known as the common alder or black alder, is a deciduous tree native to Europe, western Asia, and northern Africa. It thrives in moist soils, growing up to 30 meters (98 feet) tall, with dark gray fissured bark and sticky young shoots. The leaves are rounded with serrated edges, remaining green late into autumn. A monoecious species, it produces pendulous male catkins and upright female flowers that develop into small, woody cones.  

The tree forms a symbiotic relationship with the nitrogen-fixing bacterium *Frankia alni*, improving soil fertility and making it a pioneer species in ecological succession. It supports diverse wildlife, including insects, birds, and mammals, but is susceptible to diseases like *Phytophthora alni*. While beneficial in its native range, it can become invasive in regions like New Zealand and the U.S. Midwest.  

Its wood, though soft, is durable underwater and historically used in construction, including Venetian foundations. The bark has been used in tanning and dyeing, while extracts from its seeds show antibacterial properties. The tree is also cultivated for flood control, windbreaks, and ornamental purposes.

Terranova is a German band based between Berlin and Paris made up of current members Fetisch and &me. Terranova are signed to Kompakt records. Terranova was formed in 1996 by Fetisch, Marco Meister and Kaos. They previously went under the moniker Turntable Terranova on the Compost label and sometimes as Edition Terranova. Their music is a fusion of punk rock, electronic, hip hop and House. They currently reside between Paris and Berlin and perform DJ sets worldwide. They have been constant collaborators with artists in the past, Cath Coffey, Nicolette Krebitz, Tricky, Ari Up, Mike Ladd, Khan, Snax, Billie Ray Martin e.a.

Lydia Sokolova (1896–1974) was an English ballerina. She trained at the Stedman Ballet Academy and learned from accomplished dancers including Anna Pavlova and Enrico Cecchetti, and was a prominent member of Sergei Diaghilev's Ballets Russes from 1913 to 1929. After the disbandment of that company, she taught, choreographed and occasionally danced. Her last stage performances were in 1962 at the Royal Opera House, London.

*Unleashed* (also known as *Danny the Dog*) is a 2005 action drama film directed by Louis Leterrier and written by Luc Besson. Starring Jet Li as a man raised like an attack dog by a crime boss (Bob Hoskins), the film follows his struggle for humanity after being taken in by a blind piano tuner (Morgan Freeman). It received generally positive reviews and grossed $50.9 million worldwide.
Peter Heinrich Lambert von Hess (29 July 1792 – 4 April 1871) was a German painter, known for historic paintings, especially of the Napoleonic Wars and the Greek War of Independence.

"Bizarre Love Triangle" is a song by the English rock band New Order, released as a single in 1986 from their fourth studio album, Brotherhood (1986), which reached the top five on the US Hot Dance Music/Club Play Singles chart, and No. 5 on the Australian ARIA Charts (No. 1 on the Victoria state chart) in March 1987.

It failed to make the top 40 in either the United Kingdom (only reaching No. 56) or the US Billboard Hot 100. In the United States, the song reached No. 8 on the Hot Dance Music/Maxi-Singles Sales chart, but failed to chart on the Hot 100 during its original 1986 release. However, a new mix included on The Best of New Order was released in 1994 and finally made a brief appearance on the Billboard Hot 100 in the No. 98 position in 1995.

In 2004, the song was ranked No. 204 in Rolling Stone's "The 500 Greatest Songs of All Time."

The Meaning of Life is an Irish television programme, broadcast on RTÉ One. Presented by Gay Byrne, each edition involves the veteran broadcaster interviewing a prominent public figure.

Interviews with former Taoiseach Bertie Ahern and actors Gabriel Byrne and Brenda Fricker during the second series attracted media attention. Ahern spoke of his religious habits, Byrne and Fricker of being sexually abused as children.

Michael Cobbins (born August 9, 1992) is an American professional basketball player for Basket Brescia Leonessa of the Italian Lega Basket Serie A (LBA). He played college basketball for Oklahoma State.

Mount Hope is a 202-metre (663 ft) mountain in Victoria, Australia, first ascended by explorer Thomas Livingstone Mitchell in 1836. He discovered new plant species and noted the surrounding landscape, including Pyramid Hill. Later visited by drovers and the Burke and Wills expedition, the area became a pastoral station in 1845. A short-lived gold rush occurred in 1857, though no gold was found. A quarry operated mid-20th century before closing due to poor stone quality. The mountain is also known for Suicide Rock, named after an 1898 laborer's death.

*The Divine Wings of Tragedy* is the third studio album by American progressive metal band Symphony X, released in November 1996. Blending neoclassical, power, and symphonic metal, the album features musical and lyrical influences from classical composers and literary works. Widely regarded as a landmark in progressive metal, it received critical acclaim and has been included in several "best of" genre lists.

Annie Brisset is a Canadian translation scholar and educator, known for her work in translation theory and sociocritique. She has held leadership roles at the University of Ottawa and the University of Manitoba and is a Fellow of the Royal Society of Canada.
Ernest Dichmann Peek (November 18, 1878 – April 22, 1950) was a major general in the United States Army, who commanded the 9th Corps Area at the beginning of World War II.

Pa amb tomàquet (Catalan for "bread with tomato") is a traditional dish from Catalonia, the Balearic Islands, and Aragon, Spain. It consists of bread rubbed with ripe tomatoes, drizzled with olive oil, and seasoned with salt. Sometimes garlic is added. The dish is commonly served as a tapa or snack, accompanied by cured meats, cheeses, or marinated fish. Its origins are debated, with some sources tracing it to the 18th century, while others suggest it became popular in the late 19th century as a way to soften stale bread. Variations exist, such as Majorca's pa amb oli, which uses a specific tomato variety. The dish shares similarities with Mediterranean staples like bruschetta and pan bagnat.

Future Strategic Tanker Aircraft (FSTA) is a British project to procure Airbus A330 Multi Role Tanker Transport aerial refuelling (AR) and air transport (AT) aircraft for the Royal Air Force, to replace older models such as the VC10s and TriStars.

After evaluation of bids the RAF selected the AirTanker consortium, owned by Cobham plc, EADS, Rolls-Royce plc, Thales UK and VT Group plc, in 2004 offering the Airbus A330 MRTT.

Ambient techno is a subgenre of techno that incorporates the atmospheric textures of ambient music with the rhythmic elements and production of techno. It was pioneered by 1990s electronic artists such as Aphex Twin, Carl Craig, the Black Dog, Pete Namlook and Biosphere.

Steven Williams (born January 7, 1949) is an American actor in films and television. He is known for his roles as Captain Adam Fuller on 21 Jump Street, Lt. Jefferson Burnett on The Equalizer, Det. August Brooks on L.A. Heat, X on The X-Files, Russell "Linc" Lincoln in Linc's, and Rufus Turner in Supernatural.

Throughout his career, Williams has appeared in numerous films, including The Blues Brothers (1980), Twilight Zone: The Movie (1983), Jason Goes to Hell: The Final Friday (1993), 22 Jump Street (2014), It (2017), and Birds of Prey (2020).

He has been nominated for a Screen Actors Guild Award and one NAACP Image Award.

Echo Lawn Estate, also known as Stonegate after the Great War, is a historic estate located at Balmville in Orange County, New York. The main house was built about 1860 and is a two-story brick dwelling in the Second Empire style. It features sweeping concave mansard-type roofs. Also on the property is a cluster of mid-19th-century service buildings, an early 20th-century formal garden, and a substantial set of Arts and Crafts inspired gateposts and stone walls.

It was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 2009.

ACE (1987–1992) was a British monthly video game magazine covering multiple platforms. Published by Future plc and later EMAP, it featured game reviews, demos, and articles on graphics and computer music.
Roderick Bradley (born 29 March 1983) is a British athlete, Gladiator ("Spartan"), and wide receiver for the London Blitz.

The 2022 Argentine Torneo Federal A, was the tenth season of the Torneo Federal A, the regionalised third tier of the Argentine football league system. The tournament is reserved for teams indirectly affiliated to the Asociación del Fútbol Argentino (AFA), while teams affiliated to AFA have to play the Primera B Metropolitana, which is the other third tier competition. The competition was contested by 29 of the 31 teams that took part in the 2021 season and four teams promoted from Torneo Regional Federal Amateur. Two teams will be promoted to Primera Nacional and four teams were relegated to Torneo Regional Federal Amateur. The season began on 27 march and ended in 13 November 2022.

"Loti" is a song by Albanian singer Elvana Gjata, released on June 10, 2021, through East Music Matters and Loudcom. A pop and R&B track with folk, trap, and rock influences, it was written by Gjata and produced by Jumpa. The lyrics depict a woman mourning the loss of intimacy in her relationship. The song received critical acclaim for its fresh sound and Gjata's vocal performance, reaching number one in Albania and charting in Switzerland. An accompanying music video, filmed in Divjakë-Karavasta National Park and directed by Flicka Elisa and Vasjen Katro, premiered on YouTube and trended in multiple European countries.

OpenDataSoft is a SaaS company founded in 2011, specializing in data sharing and visualization for businesses and governments. Headquartered in Boston and Paris, it provides tools to publish, analyze, and distribute data as open data or within organizations. The company has expanded globally, serving clients like the City of Paris, SNCF, and EDF. It has received funding from investors including Salesforce Ventures and was recognized in IDC’s 2016 Smart City Innovators report.

Anything But Conservative (Lang-fr or N'importe quoi d'autre que conservateur), also known as the ABC campaign and Vote ABC, is a political campaign which aims to defeat the Conservatives in Canadian federal elections. It encourages strategic voting.

Canadian federal elections use a first-past-the-post voting system, which allows a party with minority support among the population to win the majority of the seats in the Parliament. Given the make-up of existing political parties in the 21st century in Canada, the centre and centre-left vote tends to split between the federal NDP, Liberals and Greens, which allows for the Conservatives to win a disproportional number of seats in the parliament compared to their popular support.

It is estimated by ABC campaigners that widespread strategic voting in 2015 federal elections decreased the number of Conservative seats by over 72.
Gary McDaid is a Gaelic football manager and teacher, best known for leading Glenswilly to two Donegal Senior Football Championships and managing Donegal's under-20 team from 2017 to 2019.

General der Nachrichtenaufklärung Training Referat was the training organization within the General der Nachrichtenaufklärung (GDNA), the military signals intelligence agency of the Wehrmacht, before and during World War II. Until 1942, the work of the Training Referat was not fully exploited and only a small beginners course was in progress.

Josephine is a feminine given name of Hebrew origin, meaning "(Jehovah) shall grow." Originally a French diminutive of *Joséphe*, it gained popularity in the 19th century, partly due to Empress Joséphine de Beauharnais. Common variants include *Josefina* (Spanish) and *Josefine* (Swedish), with nicknames like *Jo*, *Josie*, and *Fifi*.

Jacob "Jake" Blankenship (born March 15, 1994) is an American track and field athlete who competes in the pole vault. He holds a personal record of 5.80 m (19 ft 1⁄4 in) for the event, set in 2015. He placed third at the USA Outdoor Track and Field Championships in 2015.

Blankenship won medals at the World Youth Championships in Athletics in 2011 and NACAC Under-23 Championships in Athletics in 2014. He represents Tennessee Volunteers team collegiately and has had top three finishes both indoors and outdoors in NCAA competition, including winning the 2016 Outdoor Championships.

Dau al Set was a Catalan avant-garde magazine published from 1948 to 1951. Founded by Joan-Josep Tharrats, it initially embraced Dadaism before shifting toward Surrealism. The publication featured works by artists such as Antoni Tàpies, Joan Brossa, and Modest Cuixart, alongside contributions from international figures like Jean Cocteau and Francis Picabia. Recognized by the Museum of Modern Art as one of the 20th century's top avant-garde magazines, it became a key platform for experimental art in post-war Catalonia.

The FIFA Women's Club World Cup is an international women's association football competition that is proposed by the Fédération Internationale de Football Association (FIFA), the sport's global governing body. The competition is currently in only planning mode.

The National Democratic Party (NDP) was a far-right political party in the UK, founded in 1966 by David Brown. It contested several elections, including the 1970 general election, where it achieved its best result in Southampton Itchen (21.8%). The party later declined due to internal divisions and competition from groups like the National Front. It dissolved by the late 1970s.

The history of the mandolin traces the development of plucked string instruments from early musical bows to the modern mandolin. Originating from ancient Mesopotamian lutes (c. 3100 BC), the instrument evolved through Persian, Moorish, and European traditions. By the 14th century, European lutes featured doubled strings, leading to the mandore and later the mandolino in Renaissance Italy. The Neapolitan mandolin, developed in the 18th century by the Vinaccia family, introduced metal strings and a bent soundboard, becoming the standard form.  

A decline in the early 19th century was reversed by the "Golden Age" (late 1800s–early 1900s), marked by global mandolin orchestras and virtuosos. Post-World War I, its popularity waned, but new variants (flatback, resonator, electric) emerged in jazz, bluegrass, and rock. Today, the mandolin remains vital in folk and classical music.

*Riffs* is a 1982 live album by American jazz alto saxophonist Jimmy Lyons, recorded in Paris with bassoonist Karen Borca, bassist Jay Oliver, and drummer Paul Murphy. The album features extended improvisations on Lyons' compositions, including "Theme" and "Riffs #1/II." It highlights Lyons' work outside his long collaboration with Cecil Taylor.

The Ministry of War (Lang-de) was a ministry for military affairs of the Kingdom of Bavaria, founded as Ministerium des Kriegswesens on October 1, 1808 by King Maximilian I Joseph of Bavaria. It was located on the Ludwigstraße in Munich. Today the building, which was built by Leo von Klenze between 1824 and 1830, houses the Bavarian public record office, Bayerisches Hauptstaatsarchiv und Staatsarchiv München.

Ahmad bin Ali Al Thani (1922 – 25 November 1977) was the Emir of Qatar from 1960 until his deposition in 1972. He succeeded his father, Ali bin Abdullah Al Thani, and ruled during Qatar's independence from Britain in 1971. His reign saw economic growth due to oil discoveries and early administrative reforms. However, his frequent absences and lavish lifestyle led to discontent, culminating in a bloodless coup by his cousin, Khalifa bin Hamad Al Thani. Ahmad lived in exile until his death in London.

Sapporo Television Broadcasting, Co., Ltd. (札幌テレビ放送株式会社, Sapporo Terebi Hōsō Kabushiki Gaisha) is a TV station of Nippon News Network (NNN) and Nippon Television Network System (NNS) in Hokkaidō, Japan. Headquartered in Sapporo, the capital city of Hokkaidō prefecture, the TV station was established on April 8, 1958. It is usually called "STV" for short, which is used as a name in a number of TV programs.

Since December 15, 1962, the company has worked as both television and radio station until July 12, 2005, when the radio section was split into the STV Radio Broadcasting, Co., Ltd. (STVラジオ株式会社, STV Rajio Kabushiki Gaisha) was established. Currently, the STV Radio is a radio station of National Radio Network (NRN) in Hokkaidō, Japan. The license of the radio broadcasting was succeeded to the STV Radio, and started radio broadcasting on October 1, 2005.

Antony Thekkek, also known as Thampy Antony, is an Indian-American film actor, writer, activist and producer. As a writer he has published books in Malayalam and English. He is the author of the books Koonampara County, Life of Ouso, Lady Biker and Vasco da Gama. As an actor, he is active primarily in Malayalam cinema. He is also the elder brother of Malayalam actor Babu Antony. He is the first Malayalam actor to own a Tesla car. The actor has won the Basheer Amma Malayalam Award for his book Vasco da Gama.

Paul H. Thomson (June 29, 1916 – May 31, 2008) was an American botanist and horticulturist known for pioneering the cultivation of rare tropical fruits in California. A former U.S. Marine, he established experimental orchards in Bonsall and Vista, successfully growing lychees, mangoes, and other subtropical fruits. In 1968, he co-founded the California Rare Fruit Growers Association, now the largest amateur fruit-growing organization in the world. Thomson also studied the succulent genus *Dudleya* and privately published a handbook on the subject. His work expanded the availability of exotic fruits in California.

Kuressaare is a town in Estonia and the capital of Saare County, located on the island of Saaremaa. With a population of 12,698 (2021), it is the administrative and cultural center of the island. The town, first documented in 1154, is known for its medieval Kuressaare Castle, a historic fortress that now houses the Saaremaa Regional Museum. Kuressaare has been a seaside resort since the 19th century and hosts cultural events such as the Saaremaa Opera Days. It is served by Kuressaare Airport and has ferry connections to the mainland.

Remnantz is a historic house in England, built around 1720. It served as the Royal Military Academy (1802–1811) before being acquired by the Wethered brewing family. Since 2007, it has been owned by the Bosley family.
Joseph Buck (born December 10, 2002) is an American soccer midfielder for the Georgetown Hoyas.

Giosue Giuppone (d. 1910) was an Italian cyclist and motorsport racer. A two-time national cycling champion, he later competed in motorcycle and automobile racing for Peugeot, winning several events before dying in a crash during testing.

WNSN (101.5 MHz) is a commercial FM radio station in South Bend, Indiana. It is owned by Mid-West Family Broadcasting and airs an adult contemporary radio format known as Sunny 101.5. WNSN is usually the market's top rated radio station according to the Nielsen ratings. Sunny 101.5 carries Delilah's syndicated love songs show in the evening from Premiere Networks. Most weekends it features 1980s hits. From mid-November to Dec. 26, WNSN switches to all Christmas music.

The studios and offices are on East Douglas Road in Mishawaka. The transmitter is off Ironwood Road, south of downtown South Bend. It is co-located with sister station 960 WSBT's tower.
Kate Elizabeth Russell is an American author best known for her debut novel *My Dark Vanessa* (2020), a critically acclaimed exploration of grooming and trauma, which became a national bestseller.

CALSTAR (California Shock Trauma Air Rescue) is a regional air medical services company serving California and northern Nevada. It is currently the largest nonprofit air ambulance provider on the West Coast.

Mam Tor is a 517-metre (1,696 ft) hill in the Peak District, Derbyshire, England, known for its prominent landslips and prehistoric hillfort. Located on the southern edge of the Dark Peak, it forms part of the Great Ridge, separating the Hope Valley from Edale. The hill is composed of Carboniferous-era shale and sandstone, with an active rotational landslide on its southeastern slope that has been moving for around 4,000 years. This landslide severely damaged the old Mam Tor road, leading to its closure in 1979.  

The summit features a late Bronze Age and early Iron Age hillfort, along with burial mounds, designated as a Scheduled Ancient Monument. A popular destination for walkers, Mam Tor offers expansive views and is part of a ridgewalk connecting Rushup Edge and the Great Ridge. The ongoing landslide continues to shift at a rate of up to 0.25 metres per year, posing no immediate threat to inhabited areas but remaining a significant geological feature.

What Wild Ecstasy (1997) is a book by John Heidenry that explores the sexual revolution in the United States, focusing on key figures such as Alfred Kinsey, Hugh Hefner, and Larry Flynt, as well as topics like pornography, feminism, and homosexuality. The book received mixed reviews, with praise for its engaging narrative and criticism for its reliance on secondary sources and uneven coverage. Some accused Heidenry of plagiarism, though the publisher defended the work as factual rather than derivative. Despite controversy, it remains a notable popular history of late 20th-century sexual culture.
Annie Brisset, a member of the Royal Society of Canada, is a Professor of Translation Studies and Discourse Theory at the School of Translation and Interpretation of the University of Ottawa, Canada.

CollabNet VersionOne is a software firm headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, United States. CollabNet VersionOne products and services belong to the industry categories of value stream management, devops, agile management, application lifecycle management (ALM), and enterprise version control. These products are used by companies and government organizations to reduce the time it takes to create and release software.

Tomita–Takesaki theory is a mathematical framework in operator algebras that studies the modular automorphism group of a von Neumann algebra with respect to a faithful state. It introduces the modular operator (Δ) and modular conjugation (J), which generate a one-parameter group of automorphisms. The theory shows that these automorphisms are unique up to inner automorphisms (Connes cocycle) and connects them to KMS states in quantum statistical mechanics. It also provides insights into the structure of type III factors and uses Hilbert algebras to establish key results, including the commutation theorem relating J and Δ.

Crown Prince Hyoryeong (1149–fl. 1170), born Wang Gi, was the only son of King Uijong of Goryeo and his designated heir. He became crown prince in 1153 but was deposed in 1170 during a coup led by Jeong Jung-bu. Exiled to Jindo, his fate remains unknown after his father's assassination in 1173.

Le Sueur County is a county in southern Minnesota, United States. Established in 1853 and named after French explorer Pierre-Charles Le Sueur, its county seat is Le Center, while its largest city is Le Sueur. As of 2020, the population was 28,674. The county lies along the Minnesota River and features rolling hills, lakes, and fertile farmland.

Bakhtyuras Shampekuly Besikbayev (Lang-kk; 1920 – 26 June 1941) was a gunner and radio operator in the 207th Long-Range Bomber Aviation Regiment during the Second World War. He was posthumously declared both a Hero of the Russian Federation and a Hero of Kazakhstan after the dissolution of the Soviet Union.

The National Democratic Party (NDP) was a right wing political party that operated in the United Kingdom during the 1960s and 1970s. The NDP sought to position itself as an early rival to the National Front although ultimately it failed to challenge the position of this group.

The Neradje Mosque (Τζαμί Νερατζέ) is a historic mosque in Rethymno, Crete, built in 1890 by engineer Georgios Daskalakis during Ottoman rule. Originally a Catholic monastery, it later served as a music school.

The Farman F.220 was a French multirole aircraft developed in the 1930s, serving as both a bomber and transport plane. Derived variants included the F.221, F.222, and F.223 (later NC.223), with improvements such as retractable landing gear and twin tails. The F.222 saw combat in World War II, including night bombing raids over Germany, while the NC.223.4 became the first Allied bomber to strike Berlin in 1940. Some later models were converted to transport roles.

"Let It Go" is a song by Hong Kong singer and actor Jacky Cheung (Cantonese pinyin: Hok Yau)(中文名:张学友)written by Roxanne Seeman, Daniel Lindstrom and Daniele Musto for Cheung's album Private Corner (2010). Cantonese lyrics were written by Kenny So (乔星 Qiao Xing). The song was released to radio by Universal Music in April 2010 as the fourth single and is the seventh track on the album.

Gregg Lee Henry (born May 6, 1952) is an American character actor and rock, blues and country musician.

He is best known for his performance as serial killer Dennis Rader in the made-for-television film The Hunt for the BTK Killer, and for playing various "heavies" in various films, such as in Payback (1999) and Brian De Palma's Body Double (1984), the latter of whom Henry has collaborated with frequently over the years, acting in six De Palma films.

The 1910 Deperdussin monoplane, is a general term for a variety of Deperdussin aircraft models built between 1910 and 1911. Initially released in August 1910, it was the first aircraft to be built in significant quantities by Aéroplanes Deperdussin. Many variations of the aircraft were produced, differing in size, engine type and horsepower and tail configuration. These were loosely labelled as the "Deperdussin monoplane", but were sometimes more specifically classified either by function or by seating-type. A number of Deperdussin monoplanes were flown successfully in air races and gained several speed and endurance records during 1911–1912. Several have survived, including an airworthy example in the Shuttleworth Collection in England.

The karbovanets (Ukrainian: карбованець) was the official currency of Ukraine in three distinct periods: the Ukrainian People's Republic (1917–1920), the Nazi occupation (1942–1945), and post-Soviet Ukraine (1992–1996). Initially introduced by the Central Rada, it was later replaced by the Soviet ruble before being revived under German rule. After Ukraine's independence, the karbovanets temporarily replaced the ruble but suffered hyperinflation, leading to its replacement by the hryvnia in 1996 at a rate of 100,000 karbovantsiv = 1 hryvnia.

Romy Simpkins (born 2 November 1993) is a British beauty pageant titleholder and mental health advocate. She won Miss Dorset 2015, Miss International United Kingdom 2016, and Miss Supranational England 2018, representing the UK in international pageants. Simpkins has also appeared in television and modeling, including *Dinner Date* and commercials.

The 2018 New Hampshire elections were held on November 6, 2018, to elect state and federal officials. Incumbent Republican Governor Chris Sununu won reelection, while Democrats gained control of the Executive Council (3–2), the State Senate (14–10), and the State House of Representatives (234–166). Both of New Hampshire's U.S. House seats remained Democratic. No U.S. Senate election occurred, as the state's Class 2 seat was not up for election.

The Christmas in Spiceworld Tour was a 1999 concert tour by the Spice Girls, featuring eight shows in the UK. It included elaborate staging, a mix of their hits and new songs, and was broadcast live on Sky One. A crew member died in an accident after the final show.
Eric Records is an American record company that produced singles from 1968 to 1996 mainly with reissues of successful oldies, continuing today with compact disc compilations.

USS Plunger (SS-2) was one of the earliest submarines of the United States Navy. She was the lead boat of her class and was later renamed A-1 when she was designated an A-type submarine. She is not to be confused with the experimental submarine Plunger which was evaluated by the U.S. Navy from 1898 to 1900, but not accepted or commissioned.

The 2007 World Indoor Lacrosse Championship was the second edition of the tournament, held from May 14–20 in Halifax, Canada. Eight teams competed in a round-robin group stage, with Canada and the Haudenosaunee (Iroquois Nationals) advancing undefeated. The final between Canada and the Haudenosaunee went into overtime after a 14–14 tie in regulation, with Canada securing a 15–14 victory for their second consecutive gold medal. The United States took bronze, while England finished fourth. The tournament featured 20 games, with the Czech Republic and Ireland finishing winless in group play.

The E3 Tug Project is an initiative by Smit International and Damen Shipyards to develop next-generation tugboats based on three principles: Efficiency, Environmental sustainability, and Economic viability. The project involves three stages: analyzing existing tugs (like the *Smit Elbe*), designing an improved propulsion system for the Damen ASD Tug 2810, and conceptualizing a future tug using emerging technologies such as LNG power. Research includes collaboration with the Technological University of Delft to optimize performance and reduce emissions.
Cipher runes are encrypted runic inscriptions, primarily preserved in Icelandic manuscripts, that use structured patterns to conceal messages.
Mike Albert is an American guitarist. He has collaborated with various musicians like Frank Zappa, Don Felder, Ruben Guevara, and L. Shankar, and toured with bands like Megadeth and El Chicano.
Ruby is a deep red color named after the gemstone. Its first recorded use in English was in 1572. Variations include rubine red, ruber, and antique ruby.

Speshow is the fourth Mandarin studio album of Taiwanese Mandopop artist Show Lo (Chinese: 羅志祥). It was released on 17 November 2006 by Avex Taiwan. The name Speshow is a pun of 'Special' and his name 'Show', as he hoped it will be special for his fans. This is the last studio album of Show's, released by Avex Taiwan before his move to EMI Music Taiwan.

It was available on pre-order and four editions were released, which includes Speshow (Happiness Hunter MV Celebration Edition) (Speshow 幸福獵人影音慶功版), Speshow (Corner with Love Special Edition) (Speshow 愛*轉角 冠軍特別版) and Speshow (King's Game Live Gold Edition) (Speshow 國王遊戲LIVE黃金版) with 60 minutes of highlight live footage from Show Lo BenQ Speshow Concert at Taipei NanGang101.

Kate Walsh (née Shimmin; born 13 February 1992) is a retired Australian-English netball player who played as a goal defence and goal keeper. She spent most of her domestic career with the Adelaide Thunderbirds, winning the 2013 premiership, and also played for the Queensland Firebirds and Sunshine Coast Lightning. Walsh represented Australia in Fast5 netball (2012–2017) and later switched to play for England in 2019. She retired from elite netball in 2022 after a career spanning over a decade.

Shade is a 2003 American neo-noir crime thriller film directed and written by Damian Nieman and starring Stuart Townsend, Gabriel Byrne, Thandiwe Newton, Jamie Foxx, Melanie Griffith and Sylvester Stallone. The film follows a trio of grifters who attempt to set up a legendary card shark nicknamed "The Dean". The film had a limited release in the United States on May 7, 2004.

The National Trust for Scotland for Places of Historic Interest or Natural Beauty, commonly known as the National Trust for Scotland (Lang-gd), is a Scottish conservation organisation. It is the largest membership organisation in Scotland and describes itself as "the conservation charity that protects and promotes Scotland's natural and cultural heritage for present and future generations to enjoy".

Systems of Survival: A Dialogue on the Moral Foundations of Commerce and Politics is a 1992 book written by American urban activist Jane Jacobs.

It describes two fundamental and distinct ethical systems, or syndromes as she calls them: that of the Guardian and that of Commerce. She argues that these supply direction for the conduct of human life within societies, and understanding the tension between them can help us with public policy and personal choices.

Madden NFL 25 is a 2013 American football video game developed by EA Tiburon and published by EA Sports. Released for PlayStation 3, Xbox 360, PlayStation 4, Xbox One, iOS, and Android, it marked the 25th anniversary of the *Madden NFL* series. The game introduced the *Ignite* engine for next-gen consoles, improving physics, animations, and AI. New features included team relocation in *Connected Franchise* mode and enhanced running mechanics. Hall of Fame running back Barry Sanders appeared on the cover for PlayStation 3 and Xbox 360, while Adrian Peterson was featured on next-gen versions. Reception was mixed, with praise for gameplay improvements but criticism for repetitive commentary and freemium elements in mobile versions. The game sold over 3 million copies.

Alfred John Webb (10 June 1834 – 30 July 1908) was an Irish Quaker from a family of activist printers. He became an Irish Parliamentary Party politician and Member of Parliament (MP), as well as a participant in nationalist movements around the world. He supported Butt's Home Government Association and the United Irish League. At Madras in 1894, he became the third non-Indian (after George Yule and William Wedderburn) to preside over the Indian National Congress.

Christian Poncelet (24 March 1928 – 11 September 2020) was a conservative French politician. A member of President Nicolas Sarkozy's Union for a Popular Movement (UMP), he was President of the Senate from 1998 to 2008. In addition to being a Senator, he was Mayor of Remiremont (Vosges) and has been the President of the General Council of Vosges.
Tyrone Kirk (born 2 January 1984) is an English former professional footballer who played as a midfielder.

Jasmina Obradović is a Serbian economist and politician who served in the Novi Sad city assembly (2012–2016) and the National Assembly of Serbia (2012–present) as a member of the Progressive Party. She has held multiple committee roles and led parliamentary friendship groups.
Tateyama Station (館山駅, Tateyama-eki) is a passenger railway station in the city of Tateyama, Chiba Prefecture, Japan, operated by the East Japan Railway Company (JR East).

Christmas in Spiceworld Tour was the second concert tour by British girl group the Spice Girls. The eight-show tour was launched following "solo projects, marriages, motherhood and another round of slagging in the press", as a reunion for the girls.
Tateyama Station (館山駅) is a railway station in Tateyama, Chiba, Japan, operated by JR East. It serves the Uchibō Line and has one side platform and one island platform.

Meckenbach is a municipality in the Bad Kreuznach district of Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany. Located in the Nahe region, it has a population of around 360. The village has historical ties to copper mining and was once part of the Waldgravial Amt of Kyrburg. It features an Evangelical church with a historic Stumm organ.
The Weatherly Model 201 is a 1960s American agricultural monoplane designed and built by the Weatherly Aviation Company of Hollister, California.

*The Curse of Oak Island* is a Canadian reality television series that premiered on History Canada in 2014. The show follows brothers Rick and Marty Lagina as they search for treasure and historical artifacts on Oak Island, exploring theories about the island's mysterious "money pit" and other sites. The series features a team of researchers, including longtime treasure hunters, and examines various speculative claims about the island's past. Produced by Prometheus Entertainment, the show has aired over 160 episodes across ten seasons.

Cai Jinbiao (born 1954) is a Chinese former footballer who played as a defender for Guangdong FC and the China national team. Nicknamed "Steel Head," he helped Guangdong win their first league title in 1979.

Dardanella was a touring theatre company from the Dutch East Indies (modern-day Indonesia) established by Willy A. Piedro in 1926. Arising from a background of musical theatre, the troupe focused on realistic stories, both adaptations of foreign works and original stage plays about life in the Indies. Starring Dewi Dja' and Tan Tjeng Bok, the troupe performed original works by Piedro and Andjar Asmara. Popular both in the Indies and abroad, Dardanella dissolved during an international tour after 1936. Several of its members later went into film.

Richard Franz Stöhr (11 June 1874 – 11 December 1967) was an Austrian composer, music author and teacher.

Born in Vienna, Stöhr studied composition with Robert Fuchs at the Vienna Conservatory. After working there as a repetiteur and choral instructor from 1900, he taught music (theory of harmony, counterpoint, form) from 1903 to 1938, being professor from 1915. Among his students were Herbert von Karajan, Rudolf Serkin, Erich Leinsdorf, Samuel Barber, Erich Zeisl, Louis Horst, Marlene Dietrich, Alois Hába, Hellmut Federhofer, and Mimi Wagensonner. Fired from the Vienna Conservatory due to his Jewish heritage in 1938, he emigrated to the US in 1939 and taught at the Curtis Institute of Music in Philadelphia. His students there included Leonard Bernstein and Eugene Bossart. From 1941 to 1950 he taught at St. Michael's College in Vermont, where he maintained emeritus status until 1960. He died in Montpelier, Vermont in the United States.

Unleashed (Lang-fr) is a 2005 action thriller film directed by Louis Leterrier, written by Luc Besson, and co-produced by Jet Li and Besson. It stars Jet Li, Morgan Freeman, Bob Hoskins and Kerry Condon, with action choreography by Yuen Woo-Ping. The film's setting and shooting location are Glasgow.
Giorgos Fountas (Lang-el; February 13, 1922 – November 28, 2010) was a Greek actor in film and television.
Acadia Pharmaceuticals is a biopharmaceutical company specializing in central nervous system disorders.

Bertrand Ndongo (born c. 1990) is a Cameroonian activist for the Spanish political party Vox. He has been nicknamed in the media and in self-reference as "El Negro de Vox" (Vox's Black Guy). Since 2019, he has been a consultant to Rocío Monasterio, the party's leader in the Community of Madrid.

Kadampanad grama panchayath is the southernmost part of Pathanamthitta district. It comes under Parakkode block of Adoor taluk. The panchayath shared boundaries with Erathu panchayath in the north, Ezhamkulam panchayath in the east, Kunnathur panchayath and Kalladayar in the south and Pallikkal and Poruvazhy Panchayaths in the west. Kakkathikkunnu in the north and Pandimalakkunnu (Pandimalappuram) in the south stands as two strong forts in this panchayath which balances the climatic conditions of this area.

Kadampanad, Kallukuzhi, Thuvayoor and Mannadi areas form Kadampanad panchayath. Mannadi is known as the place where Veluthambi Dalawa, the great freedom fighter, died.
Gary McDaid is a Gaelic football manager, referee and former player. He has three DSFCs: twice as manager (2011, 2013) and once as coach to Michael Canning (2016).

The Satanita AC 59 is an English contract law case, decided in the Court of Appeal, which concerned the formation of a contract. It is notable because it stands as an example of a case which does not fit the typical pattern of offer and acceptance that English law purports to require to find agreement.

Han Lao Da (Chinese: 韩劳达; pinyin: Hán Láodá) is a Singaporean playwright, as well as founder and principal of Han Language Centre. Han received the Cultural Medallion for his contributions in the Singaporean drama scene, and is also recognised for his xiangsheng contributions in Singapore.

Yamada Amy (山田 詠美, Yamada Eimi) born February 8, 1959, is a popular but controversial contemporary Japanese writer who is most famous for her stories that address issues of sexuality, racism, and interracial love and marriage. Her debut and subsequent popular success in the 1990s was a part of Japan's hip-hop and Black culture boom. While she is most known for her stories of complicated and messy romantic love, she also writes on the daily minutiae of life (slice-of-life), child-raising, and bullying.

Sandy Saddler (June 23, 1926 – September 18, 2001) was an American professional boxer who held the world featherweight title multiple times. Best known for his fierce four-fight rivalry with Willie Pep, Saddler was a powerful puncher, winning 104 of his 145 victories by knockout. After retiring due to an eye injury, he became a trainer, working with George Foreman. Inducted into the International Boxing Hall of Fame in 1990, he is ranked among the greatest punchers in boxing history.

Maximum PC, formerly known as boot, is an American magazine and website published by Future US. It focuses on cutting-edge PC hardware, with an emphasis on product reviews, step-by-step tutorials, and in-depth technical briefs. Component coverage areas include CPUs, motherboards, core-logic chipsets, memory, videocards, mechanical hard drives, solid-state drives, optical drives, cases, component cooling, and anything else to do with recent tech news. Additional hardware coverage is directed at smartphones, tablet computers, cameras and other consumer electronic devices that interface with consumer PCs. Software coverage focuses on games, anti-virus suites, content-editing programs, and other consumer-level applications.

Prior to September 1998, the magazine was called boot. boot and sister magazine MacAddict (now Mac|Life) launched in September 1996, when Future US shut down CD-ROM Today.

In March 2016, Future US announced that the Maximum PC website would be merged with PCGamer.com, appearing as the hardware section of the website from that point forward. The magazine was not affected by this change. As of July 2, 2018, browsing to MaximumPC.com no longer forwards to the Hardware section of PCGamer.com

Crown Prince Hyoryeong (born 4 June 1149), personal name Wang Gi was a Goryeo Royal Prince as the first and oldest son of King Uijong and Queen Janggyeong who later deposed from his position alongside his father and his only son by Jeong Jung-bu and Yi Ui-bang in 1170.

Mystic Comics is the name of three comic book series published by the company that eventually became Marvel Comics. The first two series were superhero anthologies published by Marvel's 1930-1940s predecessor, Timely Comics, during what fans and historians call the Golden Age of comic books. The third, simply titled Mystic, was a horror fiction-suspense anthology from Marvel's 1950s forerunner, Atlas Comics.

Abd al-Malik ibn Umar ibn Marwan ibn al-Hakam (Lang-ar; c. 718– c. 778), also known as al-Marwani, was an Umayyad prince, general and governor of Seville under the first Umayyad emir of al-Andalus (Islamic Spain), Abd al-Rahman I (r. 756–788). He led two major campaigns in 758 and 774, the first against the previous ruler of al-Andalus Yusuf ibn Abd al-Rahman al-Fihri and the second against the rebellious troops of Seville and Beja. His victories solidified the Umayyad emirate's control of western al-Andalus. His descendants continued to play important political and military roles in the Emirate well into the 10th century.

The supine is a grammatical form found in various languages, typically functioning as a verbal noun. In Latin, it appears in two forms: the first supine (ending in *-tum*) expresses purpose after verbs of motion, while the second (ending in *-ū*) modifies adjectives. Similar constructions exist in Sanskrit, Germanic languages (such as English's to-infinitive), Finnic languages, Romance languages (like Romanian), Slavic languages, and Baltic languages (such as Lithuanian). Its usage varies but often relates to purpose or verbal action.

The 2014 Vodacom Cup was the 17th edition of South Africa's domestic rugby union competition, featuring 16 teams divided into Northern and Southern sections. Griquas won their fifth title, defeating the Golden Lions in the final. The tournament, held from 7 March to 16 May, reintroduced a quota system requiring seven black players per squad. JC Roos was the top scorer (110 points), while Devon Williams and George Tossel led with nine tries each.

The terpene synthase C-terminal domain is a protein domain involved in the cyclization of geranyl diphosphate during terpenoid biosynthesis. It stabilizes reactive intermediates and is found in terpene synthases (TPS), a gene family divided into subgroups (Tpsa-Tpsf). Structurally, it contains at least two alpha helices and is conserved across mono-, sesqui-, and diterpene synthases.

Club Deportivo Corporación Arturo Fernández Vial is a football club in Chile, from the Concepción area, in the Bio-Bio Region. The team was founded on June 3, 1903, and they currently play in the second level of Chilean football, the Primera B.

Arturo Fernández Vial identifies itself since its beginning with the national corporation of railroad workers, and it was the most popular team in southern Chile. Its traditional rival is Deportes Concepción. Also, the team's "hinchada" (die-hard supporters) are known as La Furia Guerrera.

The 2022 Torneo Federal A was the 37th season of Argentina's third-tier football league. The competition featured 34 teams divided into two zones, with each team playing home and away matches in the first stage. The top eight teams from each zone advanced to the final knockout stages, where Racing de Córdoba won the title, earning promotion to Primera Nacional. The tournament saw 559 matches played, with 1,151 goals scored. The bottom two teams from each zone were relegated to the Torneo Regional Federal Amateur. Notable results included Olimpo's 5–0 win over Sportivo Peñarol and Racing de Córdoba's 4–0 away victory over Juventud Antoniana.

Senegalese Americans are an ethnic group of Americans of Senegalese descent. In the surveys of 2019, 18,091 people claimed to be of Senegalese origin or descent in the United States. However, many West Africans trafficked by enslavers to the United States were also of Senegalese origin (arriving together with Africans of other origins who came by way of Senegalese ports). Thus many African Americans may also have some ancestors of this country.
Eric Records is an American reissue record label founded in 1968 by Bill Buster in Philadelphia, specializing in re-releasing classic pop songs from the 1950s to 1970s.

Dau al set was a Catalan avant-garde magazine founded in September 1948 by Joan-Josep Tharrats and directed by Joan Ponç. The magazine was produced by the artistic group Dau al Set. It was the continuation of another magazine called Algol (magazine) published by the same group. The magazine had eight pages measuring 250 mm × 175 mm (9.8 in × 6.9 in). The texts were written in Catalan and Spanish. The magazine continued publishing numbers until December 1951.
Peter von Hess (29 July 1792 – 4 April 1871) was a German painter known for his battle scenes and historical works, particularly depictions of the Napoleonic Wars and the Greek War of Independence.

Kalavari Samsaram (transl. Wealthy Family) is a 1982 Telugu-language drama film, produced by Donepudi Brahmaiah under the Maheswari Combines banner and directed by K. S. Rami Reddy. It stars Krishna and Sridevi, with music composed by K. V. Mahadevan. The film is based on Maadireddy Sulachana's Agni Pariksha (Novel).

The Tettidia gens was a Roman family of Oscan origin, likely derived from the nomen *Tettius*. Most inscriptions of the gens are found in Samnium, and they used common praenomina, including *Numerius*.

Systems of Survival (1992) is a non-fiction book by Jane Jacobs that examines two distinct ethical systems—Guardian Moral Syndrome and Commercial Moral Syndrome—each governing different human activities. The Guardian Syndrome, rooted in territorial and hierarchical structures, emphasizes values like loyalty and tradition, while the Commercial Syndrome supports trade and innovation with principles like competition and honesty. Jacobs argues that mixing these systems leads to dysfunction, presenting her ideas through a Socratic dialogue. The book explores their historical origins, cultural impacts, and inherent conflicts.
Robert C. Morgan (born 1943) is an American art critic, art historian, curator, poet, and artist.

The Catskill Mountain House was a historic hotel built in 1823 on a scenic plateau in New York's Catskill Mountains. A premier resort of the 19th century, it attracted artists and writers, including Thomas Cole and Washington Irving. After a rivalry with the nearby Kaaterskill Hotel, it declined in the early 20th century and was demolished in 1963. Today, only ruins and its famous views remain.

The Murakumo-class destroyer was a class of six torpedo boat destroyers of the Imperial Japanese Navy, built in England and commissioned between 1898 and 1899. Based on Thornycroft's "Thirty Knotters," they served in the Russo-Japanese War and World War I before being decommissioned by 1925.
John William Goode Jr. (March 10, 1923 – February 5, 1994) was a lawyer in San Antonio who was a figure in the 1950s and 1960s rebirth of the Republican Party in Texas.

Konanakunte is a neighborhood in southern Bangalore, Karnataka, India. Once a rural area known for farming, it has transformed into a semi-urban residential locality. As of 2001, it had a population of 13,262 and a literacy rate of 77%. The area faces infrastructure challenges despite rapid urbanization.

The Sumunkur Ainu were an Ainu subgroup inhabiting the Pacific coast of Hokkaido, from Niikappu to Shiraoi. They were distinguished by unique grave markers—arrowhead-shaped for men and needle-headed for women—and other cultural differences. Historically, they clashed with the Menasunkur Ainu, leading to the 17th-century Shakushain's Revolt. After the conflict, the Sumunkur maintained closer ties with Japan's Matsumae Domain, influencing modern Ainu population distribution in Hokkaido.
Sergio Ferrer (born 1951) is a Puerto Rican former Major League Baseball shortstop.

Shaking tent ceremony is a ritual of some of the indigenous people in North America that is used to connect the people with the spirit realm and establish a connection and line of communication between the spirit world and the mortal world. These ceremonies require special tents or lodges to be made, and are performed under the direction of a shaman, or spiritual leader, who uses different practices, rituals, and materials to perform the ceremony. This ceremony is more commonly used by specific indigenous tribes long ago but is still practiced around the continent today.

Tre Motetter (Three Motets), Op. 55 (FS 139), is a set of three a cappella choral works by Danish composer Carl Nielsen, composed in 1929. Written for mixed choir (SATB), the motets set Latin texts from Psalms 38, 23, and 31. Inspired by Renaissance polyphony, Nielsen composed them after hearing a concert by Mogens Wöldike’s Palestrina Choir, to whom the works are dedicated. Premiered in Copenhagen on 11 April 1930, they reflect Nielsen’s late stylistic shift toward a Neo-Renaissance approach. The motets were published in 1931.
Dr Michael Spira (born 24 April 1944) is a British medical doctor and GP with a special interest in weight management.
Caesium bisulfate or cesium hydrogen sulfate is an inorganic compound with the formula CsHSO4. The caesium salt of bisulfate, it is a colorless solid obtained by combining Cs2SO4 and H2SO4.

Martina Carraro (born 21 June 1993) is an Italian swimmer. She competed at the 2020 Summer Olympics, in 100 m breaststroke and 200 m breaststroke. She was European champion in short course in the 100 m breaststroke at the 2019 European Short Course Swimming Championships in Glasgow, Scotland.

Latastia is a genus of medium to large-sized lacertid lizards found in Africa, characterized by long cylindrical tails and terrestrial habits. Named after French herpetologist Fernand Lataste, these diurnal lizards inhabit sandy plains, savannas, and scrublands. The genus includes 10 recognized species, some with subspecies, and they are known for their fast-running behavior and insectivorous diet.

CubETH is a Swiss 1U CubeSat mission developed by ETH Zurich to demonstrate low-cost GNSS receivers for satellite positioning in space. Equipped with five antennas and laser ranging reflectors, it aims to compute its orbit and attitude on board while validating data via ground stations. Partners include EPFL, Lucerne University, and industry collaborators like U-blox. Planned for launch in 2017, the mission was designed to last 18 months.

The mandolin is a modern member of the lute family, dating back to Italy in the 18th century. The instrument was played across Europe but then disappeared after the Napoleonic Wars. Credit for creating the modern bowlback version of the instrument goes to the Vinaccia family of Naples. The deep bowled mandolin, especially the Neapolitan form, became common in the 19th century, following the appearance of an international hit, the Spanish Students. They toured Europe and America, and their performances created a stir that helped the mandolin to become widely popular.

Although the modern instruments date to the 18th century, ancestral instruments of similar construction and range, the mandore and gittern, were used across Europe (including Spain, Italy, England, France, Germany and Poland) centuries earlier. These instruments developed from short-handled lutes that entered Christian Europe from Muslim Sicily and Spain. Muslims picked these instruments in Central Asia, calling them barbat and oud. Residents of Asia were playing them as far back as the 2nd century A.D.

"Like a Surgeon" is a song recorded by American recording artist Ciara. Written by The-Dream and produced by Tricky Stewart, the song is included on her third studio album, Fantasy Ride. It was planned to be released as the third single from the album in North America, but its release was cancelled. It still manage to chart on the lower half of US R&B charts.

The song's promotion began on MTV. During a commercial for the album, Ciara sang part of the song in a cappella.

John W. Goode (March 10, 1923 – February 5, 1994) was an American lawyer, decorated Marine veteran of World War II, and Republican politician. He ran unsuccessfully for the U.S. House in 1961 and was later inducted into the Bexar County Republican Hall of Fame.

Terry Mutchler is an American attorney and writer. She was formerly a journalist and public servant in Pennsylvania. As a journalist, she won several awards for her reporting and was Associated Press' Bureau Chief in New Jersey and Alaska. She later became an attorney, and was appointed the first executive director of the newly established Pennsylvania Office of Open Records. Her memoir, Under This Beautiful Dome: A Senator, A Journalist, and the Politics of Gay Love in America, was a finalist for the Lambda Literary Award for Lesbian Memoir or Biography.

The FIFA Women's Club World Cup is a proposed international women's football tournament for club teams. Discussions about its creation began in 2012, with FIFA exploring formats to include teams from all regions while ensuring competitive balance.

Chas Henry (born January 6, 1989) is a former American football punter. Henry played college football for the University of Florida, earned consensus All-American honors, and was recognized as the best college punter as a senior. He was signed by the Philadelphia Eagles as an undrafted free agent in 2011.

Ernest Dichmann Peek (November 18, 1878 – April 22, 1950) was a United States Army major general who served in the Philippine–American War, World War I, and World War II, earning the Distinguished Service Medal and Silver Star.
Ángel "Cucco" Peña (born September 1, 1948) is a composer, musician, singer, and record producer.

A number of locally-specific units of measurement were used in the Czech lands to measure length, area, capacity and so on. In 1876, the metric system was made compulsory; however, local measurements and old Viennese measurements were still in use.

Theodorick Bland of Cawsons (December 2, 1708 – June 1, 1784) was a Virginia planter and politician. A member of the First Families of Virginia, he served in the Virginia Senate (1776–1779) and was a militia colonel. He owned plantations along the Appomattox River and was related to prominent colonial families.

Indore Fashion Week (IFW) is an annual fashion event that takes place in Indore. It is considered a premier fashion event in central India. It is run by Storm Fashion Company (SFC) and the title sponsor is Blenders Pride.

Allan B. Hubbard (born September 8, 1947) is an American Republican political advisor and businessman who served as Director of the National Economic Council under President George W. Bush from 2005 to 2007. He previously chaired the Indiana Republican Party (1993–1994) and worked under Vice President Dan Quayle.
Andrey Viktorovich Zayakin (Lang-ru; born 23 February 1981) is a Russian physicist, political activist and journalist. One of the founders of the volunteer community network Dissernet.

USS *Plunger* (SS-2) was the lead ship of the *Plunger*-class submarines, commissioned in 1903. Built by Crescent Shipyard, she conducted torpedo experiments and training before gaining fame when President Theodore Roosevelt took a dive aboard her in 1905. Later commanded by Ensign Chester Nimitz, she was renamed *A-1* in 1911 and served as a target before being sold for scrap in 1922.

The South African Reserve Bank (SARB) is the central bank of South Africa. It was established in 1921 after Parliament passed an act, the "Currency and Bank Act of 10 August 1920", as a direct result of the abnormal monetary and financial conditions which World War I had brought. The SARB was only the fourth central bank established outside the United Kingdom and Europe, the others being the United States, Japan and Java. The earliest suggestions for the establishment of the Central Bank in South Africa date back to 1879. A select committee, of ten members of Parliament, was established on 31 March 1920 to examine the benefits to the national interest of the establishing of the central bank.

Following on the recommendations of the committee, the South African Reserve Bank opened for business on 30 June 1921, making it the oldest central bank in Africa. The first banknotes were issued to the public by the Bank on 19 April 1922. Set of ZAR notes 2012 to present R 104 000 000 000.00 and Set of ZAR notes 2018 to present 400 000 000 print.

Unlike the Bank of England, which provided the model for establishing the SARB, the SARB is privately owned.

Stone Brothers Racing (SBR) was an Australian motor racing team that competed in the International V8 Supercars Championship between 1998 and 2012. The team was formed in 1998 when Ross and Jim Stone bought Alan Jones's shares in Alan Jones Racing and renamed it Stone Brothers Racing.

The team won the Bathurst 1000 in its debut year with Jason Bright and Steven Richards. It won the 2003 and 2004 championships with Marcos Ambrose and the 2005 championship with Russell Ingall. SBR won the Teams Championship (combined points of both cars) in 2003, 2004 and 2005, the 2004 V8 Supercars Series being a 1–2 finish.

The team was based in Yatala on the Gold Coast. Their nominated test track was initially Lakeside Raceway and later Queensland Raceway.

At the end of 2012, the team was sold to Erebus Motorsport.

Cai Jinbiao (Chinese: 蔡锦标; born 1954) is a former Chinese international footballer who played as a defender. He represented Guangdong FC where he won the 1979 Chinese league title as well as playing for the Chinese national football team in the 1980 Asian Cup.

The Burrumbeet Cup is an annual Thoroughbred horse race held on New Year's Day at Burrumbeet Racecourse in Western Victoria, Australia. First run in 1891, the 1,800-metre handicap race is the feature event of the Burrumbeet Park & Windermere Jockey Club. Recent winners include Asset Hound (2015), Heisman (2014), and Gottino (2013). The race typically attracts large crowds, with charitable fundraising often tied to the event. Sponsored by Petrogas Regional, the Burrumbeet Cup is a Benchmark 78 race with a purse of $15,000 (as of 2016). Notable trainers such as Darren Weir and Glen & Barry James have had success in the event.
The Roy model is one of the earliest works in economics on self-selection due to A. D. Roy. The basic model considers two types of workers that choose occupation in one of two sectors.

Courtney Rashelle Wetzel (born February 25, 1989) is an American soccer midfielder. She played for the Western New York Flash and Portland Thorns FC of the NWSL, and the Ottawa Fury Women and Seattle Sounders Women in the W-League.

Eucalyptus largiflorens, or black box or river box, is a tree that is endemic to Australia. It has rough, fibrous or flaky bark, dull greenish-grey, lance-shaped leaves, oval to club-shaped green to yellow flower buds, white flowers and hemispherical, cup-shaped or barrel-shaped fruit.

Eucalyptus largiflorens, commonly known as black box, is a tree native to inland Australia, growing up to 20 metres (66 ft) tall. It has rough, fibrous bark, lance-shaped leaves, and white flowers. Found in Queensland, New South Wales, Victoria, and South Australia, it thrives in grassy woodlands, particularly in the Murray–Darling basin. Indigenous Australians traditionally consumed its seeds.

The Satanita was a yacht involved in the 1894 British legal case *Clarke v Earl of Dunraven*. After colliding with *Valkyrie II* during a race, its owner was held liable under race rules that overrode statutory liability limits. The case established that participation in the race constituted a binding contract among competitors. The ruling was upheld by the House of Lords.

Andrey Zayakin (born 23 February 1981) is a Russian physicist, journalist, and anti-corruption activist. A co-founder of Dissernet, he investigates plagiarism in academic dissertations and writes for *Novaya Gazeta*. He was detained in 2022 for supporting Alexei Navalny's foundation.
The Nabaganga River is a 214 km river in Bangladesh, flowing through Chuadanga and Jhenaidah districts.

Le Sueur County is a county located in the south central portion of the U.S. state of Minnesota. As of the 2020 census, the population was 28,674. Its county seat is Le Center.

Le Sueur County is part of the Minneapolis-St. Paul-Bloomington, MN-WI Metropolitan Statistical Area.

Drapers' Academy is a secondary school for girls and boys, from years 7 to 13 sixth form with academy status, located in Harold Hill near Romford, Greater London, England. It was the first academy to be established in the London Borough of Havering.

Hilal is a rapid transit station on the M1 line of the İzmir Metro in Konak, İzmir, Turkey. Opened on 22 May 2000, it is one of two elevated stations on the system. The station features an island platform with two tracks and provides accessibility for passengers. Located within the Hilal junction, it connects to the İZBAN commuter rail via elevated walkways. The station serves as a transfer point between metro and regional rail services, with direct access to nearby streets and the Hilal neighborhood.

Acacia parramattensis, commonly known as Parramatta wattle, is a tree of the family Fabaceae native to the Blue Mountains and surrounding regions of New South Wales. It is a tall shrub or tree to about 15 m (49 ft) in height with phyllodes (flattened leaf stalks) instead of true leaves. These are finely divided bipinnate. The yellow flowers appear over summer. It generally grows in woodland or dry sclerophyll forest on alluvial or shale-based soils, generally with some clay content.

A fast-growing plant, it regenerates after bushfire by seed or suckering and can quickly colonise disturbed areas. Likewise it adapts readily to cultivation and is used in revegetation projects.

The General der Nachrichtenaufklärung Training Referat was a German military signals intelligence training unit during World War II. It provided specialized instruction in cryptanalysis, radio monitoring, and cipher techniques to personnel in the Wehrmacht's signal intelligence divisions. Courses included cryptanalysis of substitution and transposition ciphers, machine ciphers, and language-specific training for intercept operators. The program aimed to address shortages in skilled cryptanalysts and improve field intelligence operations, though effectiveness declined later in the war due to organizational disruptions.

Egon Albrecht-Lemke (19 May 1918 – 25 August 1944) was a German-Brazilian Luftwaffe fighter pilot and recipient of the Knight's Cross of the Iron Cross during World War II. Albrecht claimed 25 aerial victories, 10 over the Western Front and 15 over the Eastern Front.

Nyayangal Jayikkattum (1990) is an Indian Tamil-language political drama directed by Sivachandran, starring Sivakumar, Lakshmi, and Aishwarya. The film follows the rivalry between two politicians, with music by Shankar–Ganesh.

Christian Poncelet (24 March 1928 – 11 September 2020) was a French politician who served as President of the Senate from 1998 to 2008. A member of the Union for a Popular Movement (UMP), he was first elected to the National Assembly in 1962 and later to the Senate in 1977, representing Vosges. He died in Remiremont at age 92.

Ribadesella (Asturian: Ribeseya) is a small 84-square-kilometre (32 sq mi) municipality in the Autonomous Community of the Principality of Asturias, Spain. Known for its location on the Cantabrian Sea, at the outlet of the River Sella, Ribadesella is a town that forms part of the Picos de Europa (Peaks of Europe). It is bordered on the east by Llanes, on the south by Cangas de Onís and Parres, and on the west by Caravia. Ribadesella is the home town of Queen Letizia of Spain.

On the first weekend of August, the Descenso Internacional del Sella (International Descent of the Sella) takes place; kayakers from all over the world gather to attempt the final 20 km of the Sella River in record time.
