F.C. Netanya (Hebrew: מועדון ספורט נתניה קולט כהן) is an Israeli football club based in Netanya, competing in Liga Gimel Sharon since its founding in 2012.

The Deceased Correa (in Spanish La Difunta Correa) is a semi-pagan legendary figure in folk-religion, for which a number of people in Argentina and Chile, especially among the popular classes, feel a great devotion. It has spread, in a limited way, to neighbouring countries such as Uruguay. Every year since its inception in 1840, miracles are said to have occurred at the shrine of La Difunta Correa, and thousands of people have visited there to pay their respects. The shrine is situated in the small town of Vallecito, 1160 km from Buenos Aires and 63 km from the city of San Juan.

Farma 12 - City vs Village is the twelfth season of the Slovak version of The Farm reality television show based on the Swedish television series of the same name. The show began filming in August 2020 and premiered on August 31, 2020 on Markíza.

*Stowaway* is a 1936 American musical comedy film directed by William A. Seiter and starring Shirley Temple, Robert Young, and Alice Faye. The film follows a young orphan, Ching-Ching (Temple), who accidentally stows away on an ocean liner and helps spark a romance between a playboy (Young) and a woman (Faye) engaged to another man. Featuring songs by Harry Revel and Mack Gordon, the film was a commercial success and received positive reviews, with critics praising Temple's performance. It was later released on home media.

Thomas Parr (d. 1517) was an English courtier during the reign of Henry VIII. A descendant of Edward III, he served as Master of the Wards, Comptroller to the King, and High Sheriff of Northamptonshire and Lincolnshire. He was the father of Catherine Parr, the sixth wife of Henry VIII.

Sir Thomas Parr (c. 1483 – 11 November 1517) of Kendal in Westmorland (now Cumbria), England, was a courtier and is best known as the father of Queen Catherine Parr, the sixth and final wife of King Henry VIII.

Nyayangal Jayikkattum (transl. May justice prevail) is a 1990 Indian Tamil-language film written and directed by Sivachandran. The film stars Sivakumar, Lakshmi and Aishwarya. It was released on 1 June 1990.
The Toro Rosso STR10 is a Formula One racing car which Scuderia Toro Rosso used to compete in the 2015 Formula One season. It was driven by Max Verstappen and Carlos Sainz, Jr.

End of the World (French: *La Fin du monde*) is a 1931 French science fiction drama film directed by Abel Gance. The film follows scientist Martial Novalic, who discovers that a comet is on a collision course with Earth, leading to global panic and societal collapse. As governments suppress the truth, Novalic attempts to unite humanity under a new world order before the impending disaster. The film stars Victor Francen, Colette Darfeuil, and Gance himself in a supporting role.  

Originally conceived as a much longer project, *End of the World* was heavily edited by producers, reducing its runtime from over three hours to 105 minutes. Upon release, it was poorly received by critics and audiences, with Gance later disowning the final cut. A heavily altered 54-minute version, retitled *Paris After Dark*, was released in the U.S. in 1934. Despite its failure, the film remains notable as an ambitious early apocalyptic drama and a rare science fiction work from Gance.

Youngblood is the third studio album by Australian pop rock band 5 Seconds of Summer. The album was initially set to be released on 22 June 2018, but was later brought forward to 15 June. Four singles were released in support of the album: "Want You Back", "Youngblood", "Valentine" and "Lie to Me". The album was released to positive reviews, with critics praising the lyrical maturity and the sound change from their previous work. In support of the album, the band embarked on the Meet You There Tour.

As well as appearing on a multitude of weekly and 2018 and 2019 year-end charts over several countries and receiving multiple certifications, the album's title track, "Youngblood", impacted the ARIA decade-end chart (2010-2019), which ranks the most popular songs of the decade, at number thirty-seven. In the ARIA Australian decade-end chart (2010-2019), ranking the most popular songs of the decade by Australian artists, "Youngblood" ranked at number four. "Youngblood" sold over five million adjusted copies and is the most streamed Australian song of all time on Apple Music. According to ARIA certifications,"Youngblood" is the eleventh best-selling single in Australian history. Youngblood was the most-streamed Australian album of 2018. In February 2020, the band were honored in the APRA AMCOS' The 1,000,000,000 List, for "Youngblood" having been streamed over 1 billion times.

Akhtarul Wasey (born 1 September 1951) is an Indian Islamic scholar and professor emeritus at Jamia Millia Islamia. A recipient of the Padma Shri (2013), he has served as National Commissioner for Linguistic Minorities and president of Maulana Azad University. His work focuses on Islamic studies and interfaith dialogue.
Philipp Jakob Wilhelm von Henke (19 June 1834 in Jena – 17 May 1896 in Tübingen) was a German anatomist.

Maya Aleksandrovna Ulanovskaya (October 20, 1932 – June 25, 2020) was a Soviet-Israeli literary critic, translator, and human rights activist. Born in New York to Soviet spies, she was imprisoned in the Gulag for anti-Stalinist activities before later emigrating to Israel. She translated works from English, Hebrew, and Yiddish into Russian.

The Farman F.220 and its derivatives were thick-sectioned, high-winged, four engined monoplanes from Farman Aviation Works. Based on the push-pull configuration proven by the F.211, design started in August 1925 and the first flight of the prototype was on 26 May 1932. The definitive F.222 variant was the biggest bomber to serve in France between the world wars. One variant was designed as an airliner.

Dan Beach Bradley (July 18, 1804 – June 23, 1873) was an American medical missionary in Siam (now Thailand), known for introducing smallpox vaccination, Western medicine, and the printing press. He founded *The Bangkok Recorder*, Siam's first newspaper, and advised the royal court, including King Mongkut. Despite few Christian converts, his medical and educational contributions left a lasting impact. He is buried in Bangkok.
Tyrone Charles Kirk (born 2 January 1984, in Scunthorpe) is an English footballer.
Kate Elizabeth Russell (born 1984) is an American author. Her debut novel, My Dark Vanessa, was published in 2020 and became a national bestseller.

Echo Lawn Estate is a historic Second Empire-style mansion and estate in Balmville, New York, built around 1848. Designed by architect George C. Elliott, it features a mansard roof and retains original outbuildings, including a carriage barn, gatehouse, and greenhouse, reflecting 19th-century Picturesque and Gothic Revival influences. The estate also includes early 20th-century Arts & Crafts-style gates and formal gardens. Once home to notable residents, including art collector Natalie Knowlton Blair, it was later owned by painter Gifford Beal. Listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 2009, it remains under restoration.

Bertrand Ndongo (born c. 1990) is a Spanish political commentator and former consultant for Vox. A naturalized Spaniard originally from Equatorial Guinea, he gained attention for his controversial remarks on immigration, gender, and Spanish politics. He has faced criticism and social media suspensions for inflammatory statements, including comments on sexual assault and fake news.

Grantham and Stamford is a parliamentary constituency in Lincolnshire, England, represented in the House of Commons since 2019 by Conservative MP Gareth Davies. It includes the towns of Grantham and Stamford.
Ángel "Cucco" Peña (born September 1, 1948) is a Puerto Rican salsa composer, musician, singer, and record producer.

The 2014 FedEx Cup Playoffs were a series of four PGA Tour golf tournaments held from August 21 to September 14, 2014, concluding with the Tour Championship. Billy Horschel won the FedEx Cup after victories at the BMW Championship and Tour Championship, securing the $10 million bonus. The playoffs included The Barclays, Deutsche Bank Championship, BMW Championship, and Tour Championship, with fields narrowing from 125 to 30 players.

Dick Tracy vs. Crime, Inc. is a 1941 American film serial directed by William Witney and John English. The 15-chapter Republic Pictures production stars Ralph Byrd as detective Dick Tracy, who battles a villain known as The Ghost, capable of invisibility. Filmed on a budget of $175,919, it reused footage from earlier serials and *Deluge* (1933). Critics praised it as one of the best Dick Tracy serials, with strong action and suspense. It was re-released in 1952 as *Dick Tracy vs. Phantom Empire*.

Annie Buller (December 9, 1895 – January 19, 1973) was a Canadian labor organizer, communist activist, and co-founder of the Communist Party of Canada. A prominent union leader, she was imprisoned for her role in the 1931 Estevan miners' strike and later managed communist newspapers.

Mario Vazquez (born June 15, 1977) is an American pop and R&B singer. He gained fame as a contestant on *American Idol* (2005) before withdrawing from the competition. He later released his self-titled debut album in 2006.

Ebenezer Norman (born June 10, 1981) is a Liberian activist and founder of NDHope, an organization focused on education in Liberia. After moving to the U.S. as a teenager, he returned post-war to aid humanitarian efforts. He built schools in Liberia, overcoming setbacks like the Ebola outbreak and government disputes. Norman has been recognized with awards, including the Global Goodwill Ambassador title. He resides in Denver, Colorado.

Indore Fashion Week (IFW) is an annual fashion event held in Indore, India, since 2012. Organized by Storm Fashion Company and sponsored by Blenders Pride, it features designers from across India showcasing bridal, ethnic, and western collections. The event includes runway shows with models and celebrity appearances.

"Bizarre Love Triangle" is a song by English rock band New Order, released as a single in November 1986 from their album *Brotherhood*. Written and produced by the band, the track blends new wave, synth-pop, and alternative dance, featuring sequenced instrumentation alongside Peter Hook’s melodic bass. The song was remixed by Shep Pettibone for its 12-inch version, which later appeared on the compilation *Substance*.  

The accompanying music video, directed by Robert Longo, incorporates imagery from his *Men in the Cities* series, depicting figures in business suits suspended mid-air. Critically acclaimed, the song has been praised as a synth-pop masterpiece and ranked among the greatest songs of all time by publications like *Rolling Stone* and *Billboard*. It remains one of New Order’s most enduring and influential tracks.

The 152 mm howitzer-gun M1937 (ML-20) (Lang-ru), is a Soviet heavy gun-howitzer. The gun was developed by the design bureau of the plant no 172, headed by F. F. Petrov, as a deep upgrade of the 152-mm gun M1910/34, in turn based on the 152-mm siege gun M1910, a pre-World War I design by Schneider. It was in production from 1937 to 1946. The ML-20 saw action in World War II, mainly as a corps / army level artillery piece of the Soviet Army. Captured guns were employed by Wehrmacht and the Finnish Army. Post World War II, the ML-20 saw combat in numerous conflicts during the mid to late twentieth century.

The Chicago Ballers were a basketball team based in Chicago, Illinois. The team competed in the Junior Basketball Association (JBA), a league created for high school and junior college players as an alternative to the National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA).

Kadampanad is a village in Pathanamthitta district, Kerala, India, with a population of 26,880 (2001 census). Historically part of ancient Tamil Nadu, it was later ruled by the Kulashekhara and Venad kingdoms before becoming part of Travancore. The region features diverse geography, including rivers, hills, and paddy fields. Kadampanad is known for its religious diversity, housing prominent temples like Kadampanad Bhagavathy Temple and churches such as St. Thomas Orthodox Cathedral. Tourist attractions include the Veluthambi Dalawa memorial and the Aravakkalu Chani Cave in Mannadi. The village is divided into 17 administrative wards.

Michael Cobbins (born August 9, 1992) is an American professional basketball player for Brescia Leonessa of the Italian Lega Basket Serie A. A former standout at Oklahoma State, he was a two-time Big 12 All-Defensive Team selection.

The Catskill Mountain House, which opened in 1824, was a famous hotel near Palenville, New York, and in the Catskill Mountains overlooking the Hudson River Valley. In its prime, from the 1850s to the turn of the century, it was visited by three U.S. presidents (U.S. Grant, Chester A. Arthur, and Theodore Roosevelt) and the power elite of the day.

Kwai Tsing Theatre (Chinese: 葵青劇院) is a performing arts venue in Kwai Chung, Hong Kong. Opened in 1999, it was built by the Hong Kong Government to address the lack of medium-sized civic centers in the region. Designed by the Architectural Services Department, it cost HK$468 million and features a black box theater. The venue is managed by the Leisure and Cultural Services Department and is accessible via Kwai Fong MTR station.
"Motiv8" is a 2018 hip hop song by J. Cole from his album *KOD*, featuring samples from Crime Mob and Junior M.A.F.I.A. It peaked at No. 15 on the *Billboard* Hot 100.

Riffs is a live album by American saxophonist Jimmy Lyons. It was recorded on September 13–14, 1980 at Le Dreher, a jazz club in Paris, and was released in 1982 on the hat MUSICS label. The album features Lyons on alto saxophone, Karen Borca on bassoon, Jay Oliver on bass, and Paul Murphy on drums.

Supersingular Isogeny Key Exchange (SIDH) is a post-quantum cryptographic key exchange protocol based on the mathematics of supersingular elliptic curves and isogenies. Introduced in 2011 by De Feo, Jao, and Plût, SIDH provides forward secrecy and resists attacks from both classical and quantum computers. Unlike traditional Diffie-Hellman, SIDH relies on computing isogenies (rational maps between elliptic curves) rather than discrete logarithms, making it secure against Shor's algorithm.  

The protocol involves two parties exchanging public keys derived from secret isogenies, allowing them to compute a shared secret (the j-invariant of a resulting elliptic curve). SIDH was initially believed to offer strong security, with classical and quantum attack complexities of \(O(p^{1/4})\) and \(O(p^{1/6})\), respectively. However, in 2022, Castryck and Decru developed an efficient key-recovery attack exploiting auxiliary points in SIDH public keys, breaking proposed instantiations like SIKE.  

Despite this vulnerability, SIDH remains notable for its compact key sizes (~564 bytes) and efficient implementations, including constant-time software by Microsoft. It has influenced research in isogeny-based cryptography, including digital signatures and designated-verifier schemes.

Patrick Pothuizen (born 15 May 1972) is a Dutch football manager and former defender, currently head coach of Achilles '29. He holds the Eredivisie record for most yellow cards (84) and played for Vitesse, NEC Nijmegen, and Twente.

The Ministry of War (German: *Kriegsministerium*) was the military administration of the Kingdom of Bavaria, established in 1825 as the successor to earlier war councils. It oversaw the Bavarian Army, combining administrative, judicial, and economic responsibilities under the war minister, who also served as commander-in-chief. The ministry was dissolved in 1918 after the fall of the monarchy and replaced by a Reichswehr command office under the Weimar Republic.
Grantham and Stamford is a constituency represented in the House of Commons of the UK Parliament by Gareth Davies, a Conservative.

Haemulinae is a subfamily of grunts (Haemulidae) found in tropical and subtropical waters. It includes genera such as *Haemulon*, *Anisotremus*, and *Pomadasys*, though recent molecular studies have led to taxonomic revisions, splitting some species into new genera like *Rhencus* and *Rhonciscus*. The classification remains under debate, with some authorities differing on the placement of genera like *Genyatremus*.

Annie Buller (9 December 1895 – 19 January 1973), also known as Annie Buller-Guralnick, was a union organizer as well as co-founder of the Communist Party of Canada (CPC) and manager of many CPC publications.

WNSN (101.5 FM, "Sunny 101.5") is an adult contemporary radio station serving South Bend, Indiana, and the Michiana region. Owned by Mid-West Family Broadcasting, it broadcasts with 13,000 watts of power from a 296-meter tower. The station launched in 1962 as WSBT-FM, simulcasting its AM sister station before adopting beautiful music formats under call signs WWJY and WTHQ. In 1984, it became WNSN, adopting its current branding. Previously owned by Schurz Communications, it was sold to Gray Television in 2015 and then to Mid-West Family Broadcasting in 2016. WNSN is affiliated with Premiere Networks and streams online.

CollabNet is a private software company founded in 1999 by Tim O'Reilly, Brian Behlendorf, and Bill Portelli. Headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, it provides enterprise solutions for Agile, DevOps, and value stream management. Key products include TeamForge, VersionOne, and Continuum. The company merged with VersionOne in 2017 and later with XebiaLabs in 2020, forming Digital.ai. It serves industries like finance, healthcare, and government.

Amy Yamada (born Yamada Futaba, February 8, 1959) is a Japanese novelist known for her bold, multicultural themes. She gained fame with *Bedtime Eyes* (1985), which won the Bungei Prize and explored Black culture in Japan. Her works, including *Trash* (1991) and *A2Z* (2000), often address identity, relationships, and social alienation. Initially a manga artist, she transitioned to literature, earning critical acclaim and influencing younger writers. Several of her works have been translated into English.

Jasmina Obradović (Serbian Cyrillic: Јасмина Обрадовић; born 29 January 1961) is a politician in Serbia. She has served in the National Assembly of Serbia since 2012 as a member of the Serbian Progressive Party.

PKP class ET22 is a Polish six-axle electric freight locomotive built by Pafawag from 1969 to 1989. Factory designation is 201E. With 1183 units, it is one of the most numerous standard gauge electric locomotive built in Europe. Further 23 units were built for Morocco as E-1000 class.

Romy Simpkins (born 2 November 1993) is a British actress, model, Mental health ambassador and beauty pageant titleholder who competed in several beauty pageants. She has represented her country & the United Kingdom internationally in beauty pageants. She competed at the Miss International 2016 pageant in Tokyo, Japan. Romy competed in the Miss Supranational 2018 pageant in Krynica-Zdrój, Poland.

The February 2016 Ankara bombing was a suicide car bombing in Ankara, Turkey, on 17 February 2016, targeting military personnel. The attack, claimed by the Kurdistan Freedom Hawks (TAK), killed 30 people, including soldiers and civilians, and injured 60. The bomber was later identified as Abdulbaki Sömer, a Turkish citizen, though initial government reports falsely claimed he was a Syrian Kurd linked to the YPG and PKK.  

The bombing occurred amid heightened tensions between Turkey and Kurdish groups, following Turkish military operations in Syria and previous attacks. Turkish officials blamed Kurdish militants and the Syrian government, while NATO allies condemned the attack. The incident led to political disputes within Turkey, with opposition parties criticizing the government’s response. International leaders, including the EU and NATO, denounced the bombing.

Lead Section for "Youngblood (5 Seconds of Summer album)"  

*Youngblood* is the third studio album by Australian pop-rock band 5 Seconds of Summer, released on 15 June 2018 through Capitol Records. Marking a shift from their pop-punk roots to a more polished pop sound, the album was primarily recorded in Los Angeles and features production from Andrew Watt, Louis Bell, and others. It includes the hit singles "Want You Back," "Youngblood," "Valentine," and "Lie to Me," with the title track becoming a global success, topping charts in multiple countries and earning multi-platinum certifications.  

The album debuted at number one in Australia and the US, making 5 Seconds of Summer the first Australian act with three consecutive US number-one albums. It received generally positive reviews, with critics praising its evolution in sound while noting the departure from their earlier style. *Youngblood* won the ARIA Award for Best Group and was named one of the best albums of 2018 by *Alternative Press*. The band supported the album with the Meet You There Tour and promotional performances, including appearances on *The Tonight Show* and *The Voice*.  

As of 2020, *Youngblood* remained one of the band's most commercially successful releases, with the title track becoming the most-streamed Australian song of all time on Apple Music.
Malcolm Neville Lockyer (5 October 1923 – 28 June 1976) was a British film composer and conductor.

Faerie Tale Theatre (also known as Shelley Duvall's Faerie Tale Theatre) is an American live-action fairytale fantasy drama anthology television series of 27 episodes, that originally aired on Showtime from September 11, 1982 until November 14, 1987. It is a retelling of 26 fairy tales, particularly those written by The Brothers Grimm, Charles Perrault and Hans Christian Andersen, an additional episode is based on the poem "The Pied Piper of Hamelin".

The 27th episode was a reunion episode of cast and crew titled "Grimm Party", in which in Fairytale style, celebrating the series they attended a gala in fancy dress.

The series follow a similar style to an earlier series narrated by Shirley Temple, titled Shirley Temple's Storybook, that ran from 1958 and 1961 and also featured numerous Hollywood stars in roles.

New South Wales State Heritage Register  

The New South Wales State Heritage Register is a statutory list of natural and cultural heritage items of state significance in New South Wales, Australia. Established on 2 April 1999 under the *Heritage Act 1977* (and later amendments), the register is managed by Heritage NSW and overseen by the Heritage Council of New South Wales, a statutory body that advises the state government on heritage matters. The Council, composed of community representatives, conservation professionals, and government officials, evaluates nominations based on heritage significance before recommending listings to the Minister for Environment and Heritage.  

To be listed, an item must meet one or more heritage criteria, demonstrating its importance to the history, culture, or environment of New South Wales. Government agencies are legally required to maintain heritage assets under their care. The register includes landmarks such as Elizabeth Farm, the first property inscribed. Aboriginal heritage is separately protected under the *National Parks and Wildlife Act 1974*. The register remains active, continuously identifying and preserving the state's most significant heritage places and objects.
Julie Baroh (born August 4, 1970) is an American fantasy artist.
Reliance was an American automobile manufacturer (1904–1909) known for its early side-entrance tonneau cars and later commercial trucks, acquired by General Motors.
Maestrale was the lead ship of her class of four destroyers built for the Regia Marina (Royal Italian Navy) in the early 1930s. Completed in 1934, she served in World War II.

The Council on Religion and the Homosexual (CRH), founded in 1964 in San Francisco, was an early interfaith organization advocating for LGBTQ+ rights, hosting pivotal events like the 1965 Mardi Gras Ball protest and Candidate's Night.

Josef Kiefel (2 October 1909 – 11 March 1988) was a German communist who participated in the German Resistance against Hitler and fought with Soviet partisans against the Nazis. After the creation of East Germany in 1949, Kiefel served as a Stasi officer.
Acadia Pharmaceuticals Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company headquartered in Sorrento Valley, San Diego, California.

Lydia Sokolova (born Hilda Tansley Munnings, 1896 – 5 February 1974) was an English ballerina, best known as a principal dancer in Sergei Diaghilev's Ballets Russes from 1913 to 1929. Renowned for her role as the Chosen Maiden in *The Rite of Spring*, she also performed in *La Boutique fantasque* and *Les biches*. After the company disbanded, she taught and choreographed, later publishing a memoir, *Dancing for Diaghilev* (1960).

The Toro Rosso STR10 was a Formula One car designed by Toro Rosso for the 2015 season. Driven by Max Verstappen and Carlos Sainz Jr., it scored 67 points, achieving two fourth-place finishes. The team finished seventh in the Constructors' Championship.

The Deperdussin 1910 monoplane was an early French aircraft designed by Louis Béchereau and produced by Aéroplanes Deperdussin. First flown in October 1910, it was a mid-wing monoplane with a narrow fabric-covered fuselage, wing warping for control, and a distinctive arrow-shaped tail. Early models used an Anzani 35 hp engine, while later variants adopted more powerful Gnome rotary engines. The aircraft was commercially successful, with multiple versions developed, including racing and military variants. It played a key role in early aviation, serving in flight schools and military operations, including with the French and Australian armed forces. The design evolved into the improved Type B (1911) and later the Deperdussin 1912 Racing Monoplane.

ʿAbd al-Malik ibn ʿUmar ibn Marwān (c. 718 – c. 778) was an Umayyad prince and military commander who played a key role in consolidating the rule of Emir ʿAbd al-Raḥmān I in al-Andalus. A grandson of Caliph Marwān I, he fled to Iberia after the Abbasid Revolution (750) and became a trusted general of the emir. As governor of Seville, he suppressed revolts by local Arab factions, securing Umayyad control over western al-Andalus. His descendants held prominent positions in the Umayyad Emirate of Córdoba, including governorships and military commands. His decisive victories helped establish the Umayyad dynasty's long-term rule in the region.

Death of Angira Pasi  

On May 23, 2020, 13-year-old Dalit girl Angira Pasi was found hanged in Devdaha, Nepal, after allegedly being raped by 25-year-old Birendra Bhar. Local leaders reportedly pressured her to marry Bhar, but his family rejected her and allegedly assaulted her. Suspicion arose that she was murdered, as evidence suggested foul play. Bhar’s family was accused of offering bribes to suppress the case. Public outrage led to arrests, and human rights groups demanded an investigation into caste-based violence.
Eileen Percy (August 1902 – July 29, 1973) was an Irish-born American actress active from 1917 to 1933. She later worked as a newspaper correspondent and was married to songwriter Harry Ruby.

The Sumunkur Ainu (Katakana: スムンクㇽ, literally "west in people", i.e. "Western people") is the name of the Ainu subgroup living along the southern coast of Hokkaido, traditionally from Iburi to Hidaka. The subgroup is known to have fought with the Menasunkur Ainu to the east in the 17th century. In Japanese texts, they were also called the Haekuru Ainu (Ainu ‹See RfD› hay-kur) and the Sarunkuru Ainu (Ainu ‹See RfD› sar-un-kur, "plains-on-people", "people on the plains").

Climax is a village in Kalamazoo County, Michigan, United States. The population was 712 at the 2020 census. Founded in the early 19th century, it was named after settlers' enthusiasm upon discovering fertile prairie land. Incorporated in 1899, Climax was the first Michigan community to receive free rural mail delivery in 1896. The village is located entirely on land, with no significant water bodies within its boundaries.

Antony Thekkek is an Indian-American actor, producer, and author based in the San Francisco Bay Area. Born in Ponkunnam, Kerala, he began his film career with *Arabia* (1995) and later won Best Actor at the Honolulu International Film Festival for *Beyond the Soul* (2005). He has appeared in Malayalam and Hollywood films, including *Cash* (2010). Through his production company, Kayal Films, he has produced critically acclaimed movies such as *Papilio Buddha* and *Naam*. He is the elder brother of actor Babu Antony.
Margit L. McCorkle was an American-Canadian musicologist known for her thematic catalogues of Johannes Brahms and Robert Schumann.
The Brass era Reliance automobile was manufactured by the Reliance Automobile Manufacturing Company in Detroit, Michigan from 1904 to 1907.

The shaking tent ceremony is a spiritual ritual practiced by various Indigenous tribes in North America, particularly among Algonquin, Cree, and Ojibwe peoples. A shaman enters a cylindrical tent at night, often using a pipe or songs to summon spirits. The tent shakes violently as the shaman communicates with the spirits, seeking answers for the tribe. The ceremony, first documented by Samuel Champlain in the 17th century, has been used for healing, divination, and guidance. While practices vary among tribes—such as the number of spirits invoked or the use of humor—the ritual remains a significant cultural tradition.

Lewis James Emanuel (born 4 October 1983) is an English former professional footballer who played as a left-back or left-winger. He began his career at Bradford City, making 139 league appearances before joining Luton Town in 2006. After loan spells at Brentford and injury struggles, he helped Luton win the 2009 Football League Trophy. In 2013, he was sentenced to eight years in prison for armed robbery.

Ralfi Pagán (September 11, 1946 – November 1978) was an American singer known for his contributions to Latin soul, salsa, and disco. Born Rafael Pagán in the Bronx, New York, he gained prominence in the late 1960s and early 1970s with his smooth falsetto vocals, blending Latin rhythms with soul and pop. His 1969 self-titled debut album on Fania Records established him as a favorite among young Hispanic Americans, particularly in New York and Los Angeles. He achieved national success with his 1971 Latin cover of *Make It With You*, which reached No. 32 on the *Billboard* R&B chart, making him one of the first Latino artists to appear on *Soul Train*.  

Pagán's later work included salsa and disco recordings, such as *El Flaco De Oro* (1976) and *Girl From The Mountain* (1976). While touring in Colombia in 1978, he was murdered under disputed circumstances. Though underrecognized in his lifetime, his music experienced renewed interest in later decades, with tracks featured in films like *Piñero* (2001).

Christian Malford is a village and civil parish in the county of Wiltshire, England. The village lies about 4+1⁄2 miles (7 km) northeast of the town of Chippenham. The Bristol Avon forms most of the northern and eastern boundaries of the parish. The hamlets of Thornend and Upper Town lie within the parish.

The unusual name is evidently a corruption of Christ mal Ford, Old English moel, mal being a mark: "Christ’s mal" is Christ's mark or sign, the cross. Thus the name signified "Cross Ford". Deeds from Glastonbury Abbey cartulary relate to Christmalford Manor: in AD 940 King Edmund granted Christmalford to St Dunstan, Abbot of Glastonbury. In 1086 the Abbey of Glastonbury remained tenant-in-chief of Cristemeleforee in the ancient Domesday hundred of Sterchelai (Startley). Christian Malford and the other parishes of Startley hundred were amalgamated with additional parishes to form the hundred of Malmesbury.

Maya Aleksandrovna Ulanovskaya (also known as Maiia Ulanovskie and Maria Ulanovsky) (Russian: Майя Александровна Улановская) (Hebrew: מאיה אולאנובסקאיה) (October 20, 1932 – June 25, 2020), was an American-born Russian-Israeli who, with spouse Anatoly Yakobson, participated in the dissident movement in the USSR and became a professor, writer, and translator in Israel.

Uyirmei is a Tamil medical soap opera that aired on Zee Thamizh. The series is apparently inspired by Grey's Anatomy and House. The plot revolves around the lives of some doctors, their lives, families and patients. The show premiered on 18 August 2014.First the director was Bhushan but after a couple of episodes Sundhar.K.Vijayan started to direct. Starting from Monday 24 November 2014, the show was shifted to 7pm time Slot. Its episodes ended on 30 January 2015 with 112 episodes.

The February 2016 Ankara bombing killed at least 30 people and injured 60 in the capital of Turkey. According to Turkish authorities, the attack targeted a convoy of vehicles carrying both civilian and military personnel working at the military headquarters during the evening rush hour as the vehicles were stopped at traffic lights at an intersection with İsmet İnönü Boulevard close to Kızılay neighborhood. Several ministries, the headquarters of the army and the Turkish Parliament are located in the neighbourhood where the attack occurred. The Kurdistan Freedom Hawks (TAK) took responsibility for the attack and said they targeted security forces. Censorship monitoring organization Turkey Blocks reported nationwide internet restrictions beginning approximately one hour after the blast pursuant to an administrative order. The attack killed 14 military personnel, 14 civilian employees of the military, and a civilian (as well as the perpetrator).

The 2014 Vodacom Cup was played between 7 March and 16 May 2014 and was the 17th edition of this annual domestic cup competition. This edition of the Vodacom Cup was played between fourteen provincial rugby union teams in South Africa from the Currie Cup Premier and First Divisions, as well as the Limpopo Blue Bulls and Simba XV from Kenya.

Maria José Aguiar (born 1948) is a Portuguese painter known for her Pop art-inspired works exploring themes of sexuality and gender. She was the first woman to teach painting at Porto's School of Fine Arts.

"Everything Starts with an 'E'" is a 1989 acid house song by British group E-Zee Possee, featuring vocals from MC Kinky and production by Jeremy Healy and Simon Rogers. Originally released independently after being rejected by major labels, it became an underground rave anthem before being reissued in 1990, reaching No. 15 on the UK Singles Chart. The track, inspired by the Ibiza acid house scene, samples eclectic sources—including a Ronald McDonald LP—while MC Kinky's lyrics were intended as an anti-drug message. Despite initial bans from BBC Radio 1, it became a defining track of the late 1980s rave culture and appeared on Now That's What I Call Music! 17. The song remains a cult classic, though reception has been mixed over time.

Howard Ehmke (April 24, 1894 – March 17, 1959) was an American professional baseball pitcher who played 15 seasons in Major League Baseball (MLB) for the Buffalo Blues, Detroit Tigers, Boston Red Sox, and Philadelphia Athletics. Known for his control and sidearm delivery, Ehmke pitched a no-hitter in 1923 and was part of the Athletics' 1929 World Series championship team. His most famous performance came in Game 1 of that World Series, where he set a then-record with 13 strikeouts in a complete-game victory.  

Ehmke began his career in the minor leagues before debuting with the Buffalo Blues in 1915. After stints with the Tigers and Red Sox, he found success with the Athletics, helping them win the 1929 title. Beyond baseball, he invented a large canvas tarpaulin for covering sports fields and founded the Howard Ehmke Manufacturing Company, which remains in operation. Ehmke retired in 1930 and lived in Philadelphia until his death in 1959.
Julie Baroh (born August 4, 1970) is an American fantasy artist known for her work in *Magic: The Gathering*.
Eileen Percy (August 1902 – 29 July 1973) was an Irish-born American actress of the silent era. She appeared in more than 60 films between 1917 and 1933.
Joseph Biddle Wilkinson Jr. (February 20, 1845 – October 23, 1915) was an American judge and Confederate Army officer who served on the Board of General Appraisers from 1890 to 1899.

Abdulai Osman Conteh (born 5 August 1945) is a Sierra Leonean jurist and politician who served as Chief Justice of Belize (2000–2010). Earlier, he held key positions in Sierra Leone, including First Vice-President (1991–1992), Minister of Foreign Affairs, and Attorney-General. Educated at King's College London and Cambridge, he is known for landmark rulings on Maya land rights in Belize. He later served on the Court of Appeal in the Cayman Islands and Bahamas.

Jessica Ashley Karpov (born July 10, 1992), better known as Harloe (stylized in all caps), is an American singer and songwriter, based in Los Angeles. In addition to her career as a recording artist, Karpov co-wrote and co-produced four songs on Kelly Clarkson's 2017 album Meaning of Life, including the singles "I Don't Think About You"and"Heat". She has also written for Britney Spears, Charli XCX, Celine Dion, Zayn, K/DA, Sabrina Claudio, and Olivia Holt.

Pheona Wall is a Ugandan lawyer and student leader. She served as president of the Uganda Law Society (2020–present) and held leadership roles at the National Water and Sewerage Corporation. Known for her advocacy work, she has received awards for justice and legal innovation.

Club Deportivo Arturo Fernández Vial, commonly known as Fernández Vial, is a Chilean football club based in Concepción. Founded in 1903, the club is named after Admiral Arturo Fernández Vial, who mediated a labor dispute that inspired its creation. Nicknamed *La Máquina Aurinegra* ("The Yellow-and-Black Machine"), the club rose from Chile's third division to the top tier in just two years (1981–1982), a feat unmatched in Chilean football. Currently competing in the Primera B, the club plays at the Estadio Municipal de Concepción.

Lewis James Emanuel (born 4 October 1983) is a former professional footballer who played as a left back or as a left midfield. He played for Bradford City, Luton Town and Brentford. He retired in 2010 having played more than 200 games. Three years after finishing his playing career, Emaunel was jailed for eight years for his part in an armed robbery.

Big Thunder Ski Jumping Centre was a ski jumping venue in Thunder Bay, Ontario, Canada, operational from 1963 to 1996. Originally opened as Lille Norway Ski Area, it was later expanded with K-90 and K-120 hills, hosting World Cup events from 1980 to 1991. The venue was redeveloped in the 1980s as a national training center and hosted the 1995 FIS Nordic World Ski Championships, where Tommy Ingebrigtsen set a hill record of 137.0 m (449 ft). Closed in 1996 due to government budget cuts, efforts have been made to reopen it as a training facility. Big Thunder remains notable as the only major Nordic skiing championship site in Northern Ontario.

The 152 mm howitzer-gun M1937 (ML-20) was a Soviet artillery piece used during World War II. Officially classified as a howitzer-gun, it combined characteristics of both weapon types, offering versatility with a wide elevation range and multiple propellant charges. Designed by F. F. Petrov, it entered service in 1937 and saw action in conflicts including the Winter War and World War II. Over 6,800 were produced, and its barrel was also used in the SU-152 and ISU-152 self-propelled guns. The ML-20 was praised for its reliability, range (17.23 km), and effectiveness against fortifications and armored vehicles. Captured examples were used by Germany and Finland, and post-war, it was exported to Warsaw Pact and other nations, remaining in service in some countries for decades.

Terry Mutchler is an American lawyer, journalist, and government transparency advocate. She began her career as an award-winning journalist for *The Morning Call* and the Associated Press, where she became the first woman Statehouse Correspondent for Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and Illinois. After earning a law degree, she worked in government before becoming the founding director of Pennsylvania’s Office of Open Records (2008–2013), significantly expanding public access to government records. She later represented lawmakers in high-profile cases and received national recognition for her transparency work. Mutchler is also known for her memoir *Under This Beautiful Dome*, detailing her relationship with Illinois Senator Penny Severns.

Josephine is a female given name. It is the English version of the French name Joséphine. In Greece, the female name Josephine is Io̱si̱fína and used mainly on the island of Crete. The feminine form of the name Joseph, which is taken from the Hebrew name Yosef, meaning "(YHWH) shall grow."

Machi is a Taiwanese hip-hop group formed in 2003, known for blending rap with Taiwanese influences. They gained recognition through collaborations with artists like Missy Elliott and DJ Tommy. The group's label, Machi Entertainment, became a major force in Asian urban music.

Democratic centralism is a Leninist organizational principle combining internal debate with unified action, used by communist parties. Formulated by Vladimir Lenin, it emphasizes "freedom of discussion, unity of action," requiring members to uphold majority decisions once made. The Bolsheviks institutionalized it to prevent factionalism, later applying it to Soviet governance, where lower bodies obeyed higher ones. In practice, it often centralized power, as seen in the Soviet Union and China, where legislative bodies rubber-stamped party decisions. The Chinese Communist Party and others, like Vietnam’s, still uphold it, ensuring party discipline while nominally allowing debate. Critics argue it stifles dissent, while proponents claim it ensures cohesion.

Robin Nicholson (born in Sutton Coldfield, Warwickshire) was a British metallurgist, industrialist, and government science adviser. Educated at Cambridge, he became a professor at the University of Manchester before joining Inco and later serving as Chief Scientific Adviser to the UK government. A Fellow of the Royal Society and the Royal Academy of Engineering, he held directorships at BP, Rolls-Royce, and Pilkington.
Joseph Biddle Wilkinson Jr. (February 20, 1845 – October 23, 1915) was a Member of the Board of General Appraisers.

Dysbindin, short for dystrobrevin-binding protein 1, is a protein constituent of the dystrophin-associated protein complex (DPC) of skeletal muscle cells. It is also a part of BLOC-1, or biogenesis of lysosome-related organelles complex 1. Dysbindin was discovered by the research group of Derek Blake via yeast two-hybrid screening for binding partners of α-dystrobrevin. In addition, dysbindin is found in neural tissue of the brain, particularly in axon bundles and especially in certain axon terminals, notably mossy fiber synaptic terminals in the cerebellum and hippocampus. In humans, dysbindin is encoded by the DTNBP1 gene.

Siegfried Müller is a German former sidecarcross racer who, with Reinhard Böhler, won the inaugural FIM Sidecarcross World Championship in 1980. He also competed in the FIM Cup and European Championship from 1974 to 1981.

Joseph "Sandy" Saddler (June 23, 1926 – September 18, 2001) was an American professional boxer. He was a two-time featherweight world champion, having also held the super featherweight title. Over his twelve-year career (1944–56), Saddler scored 104 knockouts and was stopped only once himself, in his second professional fight, by Jock Leslie. In 2003, Saddler was ranked number five on The Ring magazine's list of "100 Greatest Punchers of All Time". His nephew is Grandmaster Flash.

Ambient techno is a genre blending ambient music's atmospheric textures with techno's rhythmic structures. Emerging in the late 1980s and early 1990s in the UK and US, it features layered synths, dub influences, and subdued beats. Key artists include Aphex Twin, The Orb, and Biosphere, with labels like Warp and Fax pioneering its sound.

Caesium bisulfate (CsHSO₄) is a superionic conductor above 141 °C, exhibiting high proton conductivity due to structural phase transitions. It has potential applications in electrochemical devices due to its thermal stability and efficient proton transport.

Millennium Fantasy is a two-movement orchestral composition by Ellen Taaffe Zwilich, lasting approximately 20 minutes. Scored for solo piano and orchestra, it incorporates the folk song *"Come All You Fair and Tender Ladies,"* a melody from the composer's childhood. Premiered to positive reception, critics praised its dialogue between piano and orchestra and its enduring appeal. A recording featuring pianist Jeffrey Biegel and the Florida State University Symphony Orchestra was released by Naxos Records in 2010.

Mike Albert is an American guitarist known for his work in heavy metal, rock, and Latin music, including collaborations with Megadeth, El Chicano, and Frank Zappa. He has released solo albums and continues to perform.
Rod Bradley (born 29 March 1983) is an English player of American football, as well an artist and entrepreneur.

Pennsylvania Route 934 (PA 934) is a 10.9-mile-long (17.5 km) state route located in Lebanon County, Pennsylvania. The southern terminus is at a junction with U.S. Route 322 (US 322)/PA 241 in the South Annville Township hamlet of Fontana. Its northern terminus is at Interstate 81 (I-81) at the southern edge of the Fort Indiantown Gap Military Reservation. The route passes through rural areas along with the community of Annville in between. Along its routing, PA 934 intersects US 422 in Annville and US 22 in the East Hanover Township hamlet of Harper Tavern.

PA 934 was first designated in 1928 to run from US 22 (now US 422) in Annville north to Bellegrove. By 1946, the southern terminus was extended to US 322/PA 241 while the northern terminus was extended to US 22 and PA 343. The northern terminus was extended to PA 72 in Lickdale in 1963 and realigned to PA 72 and PA 443 within the Edward Martin Military Reservation in the late 1960s. In the 1970s, the north end of PA 934 was moved to its current location at I-81.
Margit L. McCorkle (born 1942) is a musicologist, music bibliographer, editor, translator, pianist, and harpsichordist.

Christian Malford is a village and civil parish in Wiltshire, England, situated near the River Avon, about 5 miles (8 km) southeast of Chippenham. The village is known for its well-preserved Middle Jurassic fossils, including ammonites and cephalopods, rediscovered in 2008. Its historic All Saints' Church, dating to around 1300, is a Grade I listed building with medieval features. A former Congregational chapel, built in 1836, was later converted into housing.  

Christian Malford lies near the former Wilts & Berks Canal and the Great Western Main Line, which once had a small station serving the village. The M4 motorway runs nearby. The village has a primary school, a village hall, and a pub, the Rising Sun. The population was 705 at the 2011 census.

Siegfried Müller is a retired German sidecarcross passenger and, together with his driver Reinhard Böhler, the first-ever World Champion in the sport.

He has also won the German national sidecarcross championship in 1980.

"Romeo & Juliet" is a 2019 hip-hop/rap song by Mozzik and Loredana. Produced by Miksu and Macloud, it was independently released on February 1, 2019. The accompanying music video, directed by Entermedia, premiered on YouTube and has garnered over 65 million views.

Stephen James Kmetko (born February 16, 1953) is an American broadcast and entertainment journalist. First working as a news anchor and reporter in the US midwest, Kmetko moved to Los Angeles, California and embarked on a 30-year career as an entertainment journalist and television news show host.

Kmetko came out as gay in 1999, becoming one of the first prominent openly gay journalists in America.

Charles Boarman (December 24, 1795 – September 13, 1879) was a career officer in the United States Navy. He entered the naval service shortly before the War of 1812 and served until 1876, subsequently retiring as a rear admiral. He held a number of important posts, both in peace and wartime, in the Mediterranean, West Indies and Brazil Squadrons and as commandant of the Brooklyn Navy Yard. He was also assigned to special duty during the American Civil War and a member of the U.S. Naval Board at Washington, DC.

After attending naval school at the Washington Navy Yard, Boarman saw service as a young midshipmen aboard USS Jefferson during the War of 1812 and later took part in anti-piracy operations in the early 1820s. He commanded a number of warships between 1827 and 1850, most notably, USS Brandywine during the Mexican–American War. In 1876, Boarman was promoted to rear admiral on the retired list and died in Martinsburg, West Virginia, three years later.

He was among several of Catholic background, such as John Cassin, Patrick McDonough, and Philemon C. Wederstrandt, to become high-ranking naval officers in the early years of the U.S. Navy. He was also, at the time of his death, the longest serving naval officer on the Navy Register with 68 years service. The Boarman family home, the Boarman House, occupied by the family for over a century, is a state historical landmark in West Virginia.
Giorgos Fountas (1922–2010) was a Greek actor known for his roles in films such as *Stella* and *With the Shine in the Eyes*.

The Gator Guard Drill Team is a precision military drill team and color guard unit of the University of Florida's Army ROTC program, founded in 1953. Modeled after the 3rd U.S. Infantry Regiment ("The Old Guard"), it performs at events such as UF's Homecoming Parade and New Orleans' Mardi Gras. The team uses M1903 rifles with bayonets and conducts color guard ceremonies for UF, the SEC, and MLB. It also maintains a 24-hour vigil annually at a memorial for alumnus Mark T. Barrett, killed in the 1976 Axe Murder Incident.

Nicole Bilderback (born June 10, 1975) is an American actress. Born in Seoul, South Korea, and raised in Texas, she was adopted through Holt International. After studying acting in Dallas, she moved to Los Angeles and made her film debut in *Clueless* (1995). She later appeared in its TV adaptation and had roles in *House* and *Mercenaries* (2014). Bilderback has also been featured in magazines such as *KoreAm Journal* and *Yolk*.

The Roy model is an economic framework analyzing occupational choice based on skill distribution and comparative advantage. Originally applied to labor selection, it has been extended to migration and labor market participation.

"Loti" (IPA-sq; ‹See RfD› transl. Tear) is a song by Albanian singer and songwriter Elvana Gjata. The song was composed and written by Gjata, while produced and arranged by Jumpa and Lex Barkey, respectively. Loudcom under exclusive license from East Music Matters released it as a single for digital download and streaming on 10 June 2021. Musically, it is an Albanian-language pop, R&B and urban song fusing folk, trap and rock elements, with the lyrics telling a tale of a woman, who loses the intimacy of her loved one. Music critics applauded "Loti", with praise concentrated on its appeal, music video as well as Gjata's musical versatility. Commercially, the song peaked atop the ranking in Albania and reached the top 60 in Switzerland. An accompanying music video was uploaded to Gjata's YouTube channel on 10 June 2021 and was filmed at the Divjakë-Karavasta National Park, Albania.
Robert C. Morgan (born [year]) is an American art critic, curator, and professor specializing in conceptual and post-conceptual art.

Maestrale was the lead ship of her class of destroyers built for the Italian Royal Navy in the 1930s. Completed in 1934, she served in World War II before being scuttled in 1943 to prevent capture following the Italian armistice.

Aadmi (transl. Man) is a 1993 Indian Hindi-language action film directed by Arshad Khan, starring Mithun Chakraborty, Gautami, Harish and Ajit Khan. The film features a score and soundtrack composed by Jatin–Lalit, while Anwar Sagar wrote the lyrics.

The Koza riot (コザ暴動, Koza bōdō) was a violent and spontaneous protest against the US military presence in Okinawa, which occurred on the night of December 20, 1970, into the morning of the following day. Roughly 5,000 Okinawans clashed with roughly 700 American MPs in an event which has been regarded as symbolic of Okinawan anger against 25 years of US military occupation. In the riot, approximately 60 Americans and 27 Okinawans were injured, 80 cars were burned, and several buildings on Kadena Air Base were destroyed or heavily damaged.
Animal Control Products is a New Zealand state-owned enterprise, established in 1991, that manufactures and distributes pest control products from its Whanganui facility.

The Show Must Go On (Korean: 우아한 세계; RR: Uahan segye; lit: Elegant World) is a 2007 South Korean action film written and directed by Han Jae-rim, starring Song Kang-ho in the lead role. The film revolves around a full-time gangster who's aspiring to be a full-time husband and dad.

The film released theatrically in South Korea on 5 April 2007.

Uyirmei is a 2014–2015 Indian Tamil-language medical soap opera that aired on Zee Tamil from August 18, 2014, to January 30, 2015. Directed by Sundar K. Vijayan and Bhushan, the series starred Amala, Praveen, and Vineeth in lead roles. The plot follows Dr. Kavitha (Amala) as she battles the heirs of a deceased hospital founder to protect the institution. Produced by Global One Studios, the show was filmed on a hospital set designed by Thota Tharani in Chennai.

The National Conference of State Legislatures (NCSL) is a bipartisan U.S. organization that supports state lawmakers through policy research, advocacy, and networking. Founded in 1975, it operates from Washington, D.C., and Denver.

End of the World (Lang-fr) is a 1931 French science fiction film directed by Abel Gance based on the novel Omega: The Last Days of the World by Camille Flammarion. The film stars Victor Francen as Martial Novalic, Colette Darfeuil as Genevieve de Murcie, Abel Gance as Jean Novalic, and Jeanne Brindau as Madame Novalic. The plot concerns a comet hurtling toward Earth on a collision course and the different reactions people have to the impending disaster. Scientist Martial Novalic who discovers the comet, seeks a solution to the problem and becomes a fugitive after skeptical authorities blame him for starting a mass panic.

End of the World was director Abel Gance's first sound film. The original film was to be over three hours long, but the backing production took the film from Gance, and cut it to be 105 minutes. It was again cut on its release in the United States under the title of Paris after Dark. Neither abridged version of the film was well received by audiences or critics.
The gens Tettidia, occasionally found as Tettiedia, was an obscure plebeian family at ancient Rome. No members of this gens are mentioned by Roman writers, but several are known from inscriptions.

The Future Strategic Tanker Aircraft (FSTA) is a Private Finance Initiative (PFI) program to replace the Royal Air Force's aging Vickers VC10 and Lockheed TriStar tanker fleet. The £10.5 billion contract, awarded in 2008, involves 14 Airbus A330-200 aircraft (designated Voyager) converted for air-to-air refueling and transport. Operated by the AirTanker consortium, the fleet entered service in 2013, with some aircraft available for commercial leasing when not in military use. The program faced delays but became the first UK aircraft project since World War II delivered on time and budget.

"Like a Surgeon" is a song by American singer Ciara from her third studio album, *Fantasy Ride* (2009). Produced by Tricky Stewart and The-Dream, the R&B track features provocative medical-themed metaphors for sex. Initially planned as a North American single, its release was canceled due to poor album sales. The song received mixed reviews, with critics praising its dark, unconventional production but criticizing its lyrical concept. It debuted at No. 92 on *Billboard*'s Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs chart and later appeared on *Billboard*'s list of the 100 Best Deep Cuts by 21st Century Pop Stars.

"Audi" is a song by American rapper Smokepurpp. It was released on May 17, 2017, as the lead single from his debut mixtape Deadstar. The track is considered one of his breakout singles, along with "Ski Mask".

Courtney Wetzel (born February 25, 1989) is an American former soccer midfielder who played for the Portland Thorns FC in the NWSL, winning the 2013 championship. She also played for Ottawa Fury Women and the United States U23 team.

Josef Kiefel (2 October 1909 – 1988) was a German communist and intelligence officer. After fleeing Nazi Germany for the Soviet Union, he fought in World War II as a partisan operative. Postwar, he helped establish East Germany's security apparatus and led the Stasi's counterintelligence department from 1953 to 1960.

Pierre Galet (28 January 1921 – 30 December 2019) was a French ampelographer and author who was an influential figure within ampelography in the 20th century and before DNA typing was widely introduced. Beginning in the 1950s, Pierre Galet introduced a system for identifying varieties based on the shape, contours and characteristics of the leaves of the vines, petioles, growing shoots, shoot tips, grape clusters, as well as the colour, size, seed content and flavour of the grapes. The impact and comprehensiveness of his work earned him the consideration as the "father of modern ampelography". He started publishing within ampelography in the 1950s and his Ph.D. thesis was presented in 1967. He has also written popular science books on grape varieties. Galet was active at the École Nationale Supérieure Agronomique de Montpellier.

In molecular biology, this protein domain belongs to the terpene synthase family (TPS). Its role is to synthesize terpenes, which are part of primary metabolism, such as sterols and carotene, and also part of the secondary metabolism. This entry will focus on the C terminal domain of the TPS protein.
